item.title: 在 Kubernetes 上运行生产级数据库：挑战和解决方案 | Running Production-Grade Databases on Kubernetes: Challenges and Solutions
item.description:
作者： Tamal Saha，AppsCode Inc 创始人兼首席执行官（KCD Chennai 2022 金牌赞助商）

随着容器接管软件开发的世界，Kubernetes 已经成为让开发人员无缝部署、扩展、运行应用程序和管理其生命周期的平台。 鉴于 DevOps 团队执行的多云环境，Kubernetes 抽象了云提供商，使企业能够构建可以在任何地方运行的云原生应用程序。

但是在容器化环境中管理有状态的工作负载一直是一个挑战。 然而，随着 Kubernetes 的发展，整个社区都在努力带来有状态的工作负载来满足企业用户的需求。 因此，Kubernetes 引入了 StatefulSets，它从 Kubernetes 1.9 版开始就支持有状态的工作负载。 尽管如此，数据层仍然要求很高，因为它需要大量知识才能在 Kubernetes 上运行数据应用程序或有状态应用程序。 到今天为止，如果你想在 Kubernetes 上运行你的有状态应用程序并且你需要一个数据库，你有三种解决方案或选项来实现它，一种是使用 AWS、Azure、GCP 等云提供商的托管数据库服务. 或者，您可以使用通常由数据库供应商自己提供的托管数据库服务，如 Elastic Cloud、MongoDB Atlas 等。第三种选择是运行 Kubernetes 集群并在 Kubernetes 中运行您的数据库。

如果您使用云提供商解决方案，主要缺点是云提供商并不是您的 DBA。 如果您的查询速度很慢，他们不会告诉您如何解决这个问题，您必须自己解决。 并且您的操作程序将成为供应商锁定。 自定义选项有限。 您不能只使用您需要的数据库扩展。 如果您有数据主权或气隙要求，那么您就不走运了。 云服务的基于使用的定价模型在规模上可能非常昂贵。 数据库供应商解决方案也存在同样的问题。 在这里，您将面临一个额外的问题，即选择多个数据库引擎。 数据库供应商只提供一个他们正在开发的数据库。

因此，您可以选择在 Kubernetes 中运行数据库。 今天的 Kubernetes 比 2014 年成立时更加稳定和成熟。但是以 Kubernetes 原生方式使用数据库需要一些准备。 你应该对 Kubernetes、Helm Chart、YAML、一些数据库引擎有很好的了解，然后根据你的需要配置这些东西。 即使在初始配置之后，您仍将面临日常数据库任务的复杂性，例如监控、升级、修补、扩展、卷扩展、备份和恢复、故障检测和安全性。 您必须手动执行很多操作才能将数据库用于 Kubernetes 中的生产工作负载。

这就是基于 Kubernetes 运营商的方法真正大放异彩的地方。 与人工操作员或 DBA 一样，数据库的 Kubernetes 操作员可以简化和自动化日常数据库任务，例如配置、监控、升级、修补、扩展、卷扩展、备份和恢复、故障检测和安全配置。

与人类 DBA 不同，您可以将左侧的数据库管理转移给应用程序开发人员。 开发人员可以使用 GitOps 在任意数量的 Kubernetes 集群中统一采用云原生 DevOps 实践。 您不再局限于云。 您可以将这些相同的实践带到目前运行大多数工作负载的本地。 您只需使用标准的 Kubernetes CLI 和 API 来配置您的数据库。 您可以将数据库升级到数据库版本的任何主要或次要更新。 您可以使用 Prometheus 指标与应用程序的其余部分一起垂直和水平扩展数据库。 您可以在 Kubernetes 中扩展数据库的存储容量。 它让您可以专注于您的应用程序，从而为它们提供所需的快速性能、高可用性、安全性和兼容性。 而且这个名单还在增长。 与云提供商和数据库供应商的竞争解决方案相比，KubeDB 与 Kubernetes 的原生集成使其成为独特的解决方案。 重要链接：

公司网站 - https://appscode.com/contact/



原文:

Author: Tamal Saha, Founder & CEO, AppsCode Inc (Gold Sponsor, KCD Chennai 2022)

As containers are taking over the world of software development, Kubernetes has emerged as the platform that lets developers seamlessly deploy, scale, run applications, and manage their life cycles. Kubernetes is a DevOps game-changer since it allows teams to focus on applications and deployment rather than worrying about the underlying infrastructure. Given the multi-cloud environment in which DevOps teams perform, Kubernetes abstracts the cloud provider and enables enterprises to build cloud-native applications that can run anywhere.

But managing stateful workloads in a containerized environment has always been a challenge. However, as Kubernetes developed, the whole community worked hard to bring stateful workloads to meet the needs of their enterprise users. As a result, Kubernetes introduced StatefulSets which supports stateful workloads since Kubernetes version 1.9. Users of Kubernetes now can use stateful applications like databases, AI workloads, and big data. Kubernetes support for stateful workloads comes in the form of StatefulSets.

Despite all of these, the data layer is still demanding because it requires a vast knowledge to run data applications or stateful applications on Kubernetes. And as of today, if you want to run your stateful applications on Kubernetes and you need a database, you have three solutions or options to implement it, one is to go with the managed database service of a cloud provider like AWS, Azure, GCP. Or, you can go with the hosted database service that is usually provided by the database vendors themselves like Elastic Cloud, MongoDB Atlas, etc. And the third option is you run the Kubernetes cluster and run your databases inside Kubernetes.

If you go to the cloud provider solution, the primary drawback is that cloud providers aren't really your DBA. If you have a slow query, they are not going to tell you how to fix that and you have to figure that out on your own. And your operating procedures will become vendor locked. There is limited option for customization. You can’t just use that database extension you need. If you have data sovereignty or air-gapped requirements, you are out of luck. The usage based pricing model of cloud services can be very expensive at scale. And the same kind of issue goes with the Database Vendor Solutions too. Here, you will face an additional issue which is the choice of more than one database engine. Database vendors only offer one database which they are working on.

So, you are left with the option of running databases inside Kubernetes. Kubernetes is much more stable and mature today than it was at its inception in 2014. But using databases in Kubernetes native way requires some preparation. You should have a good knowledge of Kubernetes, Helm Chart, YAML, some database engine and then provision those things according to your needs. Even after initial provisioning, you will face complications with routine database tasks such as Monitoring, Upgrading, Patching, Scaling, Volume Expansion, Backup and Recovery, Failure detection, and Security. There will be a lot of things you have to do manually to keep your database for production workloads in Kubernetes.

This is where a Kubernetes operator based approach can really shine. Kubernetes operators are applications that run inside a Kubernetes cluster and extend Kubernetes for a specific application. Like a human operator or DBA, a Kubernetes operator for a database can simplify and automate routine database tasks such as Provisioning, Monitoring, Upgrading, Patching, Scaling, Volume Expansion, Backup and Recovery, Failure detection, and Security configuration.

Unlike human DBAs, you can shift left database management to app developers. Developers can adopt cloud native DevOps practices uniformly across any number of Kubernetes clusters using GitOps. You are no longer limited to the cloud. You can bring those same practices to on-premises where the majority of workloads run today. You don’t need to worry about all of the knowledge of Kubernetes concept, Helm, and database configuration. A Kubernetes operator simplifies the whole process for you using Custom Resources. You just have to use standard Kubernetes CLI and API to provision your databases. You can upgrade your database to any major or minor update of the database version. You can use Prometheus metrics to scale your database both vertically and horizontally with the rest of your application. You can expand your storage capacity of the database in Kubernetes. A Kubernetes operator can secure your database using TLS and automate certificate rotation. Kubernetes operators can also protect your database from disaster by periodically taking backups to object stores like S3 etc. In short, a well implemented Kubernetes operator based database management solution can offer you the best of both worlds - you get the benefits of a cloud provider managed solution without any of its limitations.

KubeDB by AppsCode is an open-core production-grade cloud-native database management solution for Kubernetes. KubeDB simplifies and automates routine database tasks such as provisioning, patching, backup, recovery, scaling & tls management for various popular databases on private and public clouds. It frees you to focus on your applications so you can give them the fast performance, high availability, security and compatibility they need. KubeDB offers many familiar database engines to choose from, including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Elasticsearch, Redis and Memcached. And the list is growing. KubeDB’s native integration with Kubernetes makes it a unique solution compared to competitive solutions from cloud providers and database vendors. KubeDB has been under development since 2017 and is powering critical infrastructure for many enterprises in Telecom, Healthcare, Gaming, EPR, and Financial industries.

Important Links:

KubeDB website - https://kubedb.com/
Stash website (for DB backup) - https://stash.run/
Company Website - https://appscode.com/contact/
item.author: kcdchennai_user

item.title: 为您的下一个物联网项目聘请 ReactJS 开发人员的 7 个技巧 | 7 Tips to Get Hire ReactJS Developer for Your Next IoT Project
item.description:
聘请 reactJS 开发人员总是很棘手——尤其是当您不知道要寻找什么时。 在本文中，我们将介绍 7 个重要提示，以便在您完成招聘过程时记住。

什么是反应JS？
它由 Facebook 和一个由个人开发者和公司组成的社区维护。 如何使用 ReactJS？
一些公司还使用 ReactJS 作为其 Web 应用程序的基础。

使用 reactJS 有什么好处？
在开发 Web 应用程序时使用 ReactJS 有很多好处。 一个优点是它使代码易于阅读和理解。 通过这种方式，专家可以更快、更轻松地进行开发。 此外，由于 ReactJS 创建了可重用的组件，它可以在开发使用类似组件的应用程序时节省时间。 最后，ReactJS 不断更新新功能和错误修复，这可以提高开发效率。

聘请最佳 ReactJS 开发人员的 7 个技巧
在为您的下一个物联网项目聘请 ReactJS 开发人员时，有很多事情需要考虑。 这里有 7 个技巧可以帮助你聘请 ReactJS 开发人员来完成这项工作：

  1. 确保开发人员有使用 ReactJS 的经验。
  2. 询问以前工作的例子。
  3. 检查开发人员的代码质量。
  4. 确保开发人员在您需要时可用。
  5. 了解开发人员的沟通方式。
  6. 向以前的客户索取参考资料。
  7. 确保开发商愿意签署保密协议 (NDA)。

它用于开发 Web 应用程序，并以其简单性和灵活性而闻名。

使用 ReactJS 有很多优点。 主要优点之一是它使代码易于阅读和理解。 这是因为 React JS 使用声明式的编程风格，这使得代码比其他编程语言更具可读性。

这是因为它使用虚拟 DOM，这意味着无需重新加载整个页面即可对 UI 进行更改。 它将使您的 Web 应用程序更快、响应更快。

然而，使用 ReactJS 也有一些缺点。 主要缺点之一是它可能难以学习。 这是因为它使用与大多数其他语言不同的编程风格。 由于代码的编写方式，调试 React JS 应用程序也可能具有挑战性。

总体而言，React JS 具有许多优点，使其成为开发 Web 应用程序的不错选择。 但是，学习和调试可能很困难。 如果您正在寻找一种简单易用的编程语言，那么 ReactJS 可能不是那个。

如何为您的项目找到合适的开发人员（如果您是公司）？
当您正在为您的下一个 IoT 项目寻找 ReactJS 开发人员时，您应该记住以下几点：

  • 确保开发人员具有 ReactJS 经验并熟悉框架。
  • 确保开发人员熟悉您正在使用的特定物联网平台。
  • 找到一个非常适合您的公司文化并且能够与您的团队一起工作的开发人员非常重要。 有几种方法可以找到满足这些条件的 ReactJS 开发人员。

方式一- 在在线工作委员会或 Upwork 等网站上发布工作列表。

方式二- 参加 ReactJS 聚会或会议，并亲自了解一些开发人员。

方式三- 最简单的方式之一，联系 ReactJS 开发公司并询问他们是否有任何建议。

一旦你找到了一些潜在的候选人，一定要采访他们并询问他们在 ReactJS 和 IoT 方面的经验。 此外，询问他们的可用性以及它们是否适合您的公司文化。 面试候选人后，您将对谁最适合您的项目有一个很好的了解。

为什么 ReactJS 是构建交互式用户界面的好选择？
首先，它是一个非常流行的 JavaScript 库，被许多开发人员使用。 这意味着如果您需要帮助，大型开发人员社区可以帮助您完成项目。

它使用虚拟 DOM，这意味着只更新页面的更改部分而不是整个页面。 这使您的页面加载速度更快并节省资源。

如果您了解 HTML 和 JavaScript，您将能够快速掌握 ReactJS。 如果您需要帮助，可以使用许多在线资源来帮助您学习 ReactJS。

总的来说，ReactJS 是构建交互式用户界面的不错选择，因为它流行且高效。 如果你正在寻找一个 JavaScript 库来构建你的下一个项目，那么 ReactJS 应该是你的首选。

结论

在为您的下一个物联网项目聘请 ReactJS 开发人员时，需要考虑许多因素。 但是，通过遵循本文中的提示，您可以找到最适合您的特定需求和要求的 ReactJS 开发人员。 在熟练的 ReactJS 开发人员的帮助下，您可以将您的 IoT 项目变为现实，并为您的用户创造惊人的体验。



原文:

Hiring a reactJS developer is always tricky- especially when you don’t know what to look for. In this article, we will cover 7 important tips to remember when you are going through the hiring process.

What is reactJS?
ReactJS is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces. It is maintained by Facebook and a community of individual developers and companies. ReactJS allows developers to create reusable components that can be used in web applications.

How to use reactJS?
ReactJS can be used with other JavaScript libraries or frameworks, like AngularJS. ReactJS components can also be used in several ways, including in the form of a web component. Some companies also use ReactJS as the basis for their web applications.

What are the benefits of using reactJS?
There are many benefits of using ReactJS when developing web applications. One advantage is that it makes code easy to read and understand. This way, experts make development faster and easier. Additionally, since ReactJS creates reusable components, it can save time when developing applications that use similar components. Finally, ReactJS is constantly being updated with new features and bug fixes, which can make development more efficient.

7 Tips to Hire the Best ReactJS Developer
There are a lot of things to consider when hiring a ReactJS developer for your next IoT project. Here are 7 tips that will help you to Hire ReactJS developers for the job:

  1. Make sure the developer has experience with ReactJS.
  2. Ask for examples of previous work.
  3. Check the developer’s code quality.
  4. Make sure the developer is available when you need them.
  5. Get a feel for the developer’s communication style.
  6. Ask for references from previous clients.
  7. Make sure the developer is willing to sign a non-disclosure agreement (NDA).

The advantages and disadvantages of reactJS
React JS is a programming language gaining popularity in recent years. It is used for developing web applications and is known for its simplicity and flexibility.

There are many advantages of using ReactJS. One of the main advantages is that it makes code easy to read and understand. This is because React JS uses a declarative style of programming which makes code more readable than other programming languages.

Another advantage of React JS is that it is very fast. This is because it uses a virtual DOM, which means that changes to the UI are made without reloading the entire page. It will make your web application much faster and more responsive.

However, there are some disadvantages to using ReactJS as well. One of the main disadvantages is that it can be difficult to learn. This is because it uses a different style of programming than most other languages. It can also be challenging to debug React JS applications due to the way that the code is written.

Overall, React JS has many advantages that make it a good choice for developing web applications. However, it can be difficult to learn and debug. If you are looking for a programming language that is simple and easy to use, then ReactJS may not be the one.

How to find the right developer for your project (if you are a company)?
When you are looking for a ReactJS developer to hire for your next IoT project, there are a few things you should keep in mind:

  • Make sure that the developer has experience with ReactJS and is familiar with the framework.
  • Ensure that the developer is familiar with the specific IoT platform you are using.
  • It is important to find a developer who is a good fit for your company culture and who will be able to work well with your team. There are a few ways to find ReactJS developers who meet these criteria.

Way One - Post a job listing on online job boards or websites like Upwork.

Way Two - Attend ReactJS meetups or conferences and get to know some developers in person.

Way Three - One of the easiest ways, reach out to ReactJS development companies and ask if they have any recommendations.

Once you have found a few potential candidates, be sure to interview them and ask about their experience with ReactJS and IoT. Also, ask them about their availability and whether they would be a good fit for your company culture. After interviewing the candidates, you will have a good idea of who would be the best fit for your project.

Why is ReactJS a good choice for building interactive user interfaces?
There are many reasons why ReactJS is a good choice for building interactive user interfaces. First, it is a very popular JavaScript library that is used by many developers. This means that a large community of developers can help you with your project if you need assistance.

Another reason why ReactJS is a good choice for building interactive user interfaces is that it is very efficient. It uses a virtual DOM, which means only the changed parts of the page are updated instead of the entire page. This makes your pages load faster and saves on resources.

If you know HTML and JavaScript, you will be able to pick up ReactJS quickly. Many online resources are available to help you learn ReactJS if you need assistance.

Overall, ReactJS is a good choice for building interactive user interfaces because it is popular and efficient. If you are looking for a JavaScript library to build your next project, then ReactJS should be at the top of your list.

Conclusion

There are many factors to consider while hiring a ReactJS developer for your next IoT project. However, by following the tips in this article, you can find the best ReactJS developer for your specific needs and requirements. With the help of a skilled ReactJS developer, you can bring your IoT project to life and create an amazing experience for your users.
item.author: theonetechnologies

item.title: 天蓝色应用服务 | AZURE APP SERVICE
item.description:
伊丽莎白

发表于我的天蓝色博客

什么是 Microsoft Azure：Azure 是一个公共云计算平台，提供分析、虚拟计算、存储、网络等服务。 它可以增强或替换您的本地服务器。 您可以使用任何您喜欢的语言进行编码，包括 .NET、.NET Core、Java、Ruby、Node.js、PHP 或 Python。 基于 Windows 和 Linux 的系统都可以轻松运行和扩展应用程序。

应用服务不仅将 Microsoft Azure 的安全性、负载平衡、自动缩放和自动管理功能添加到您的应用程序中。 它还具有 DevOps 功能，包括通过 Azure DevOps、GitHub、Docker Hub 和其他来源进行持续部署、包管理、暂存环境、自定义域和 TLS/SSL 证书。

  1. 创建 Azure 帐户 (Microsoft.azure.com)
  2. 登录到您的 Microsoft Azure 帐户 (portal.azure.com)
  3. 在 Microsoft Azure 主页选项卡上，单击应用服务

创建新的应用服务计划

  1. 然后单击顶部选项栏上的创建（+创建）
  2. 指定您的订阅选择
  3. 单击创建新资源组

  1. 在实例详细信息下的名称部分中，添加一个唯一名称。
  2. 在发布部分，如果您要发布代码（可以使用 Visual Studio 发布代码）、Docker 容器（包含软件运行所需的所有内容的打包软件，包括库、系统工具、代码和运行时），请选择代码，或者静态 Web 应用（您可以使用快速入门在 azure 上构建静态 Web 应用）。
  3. 在运行时堆栈下拉列表中选择您的运行时堆栈。 运行时堆栈仅表示构建应用程序时使用的编程语言。
  4. 选择您的操作系统，Windows 或 Linux

  5. 选择最适合您的应用服务计划的地区

  6.  Click on review and create, review your actions and create if all is set up.


    欢呼！！ 您的应用服务已全部设置完毕。

在我的下一篇博客中，我将分享有关如何在 Azure 上构建静态 Web 应用程序的见解。 我希望你和我一样兴奋。

亚历山大·卡默勒 - Apr 3 4 月 3 日



原文:

lizabeth

Posted on May 16

MY AZURE BLOG

What is Microsoft Azure: Azure is a public cloud computing platform that offers services such as analytics, virtual computing, storage, networking, and more. It can enhance or replace your on-premise servers. Microsoft Azure is Flexible (you can move to compute resources up and down as needed), Open (Supports almost any OS, language, tool, or framework), Reliable (99.95% availability SLA and 24×7 tech support), Global (Data housed in geosynchronous data centers), and Economical (you Only pay for what you use) In this article, we will be creating an app service on Microsoft azure. Azure App Service is a one-of-a kind cloud service that allows you to rapidly and easily build enterprise-ready web and mobile apps for any platform or device, then deploy them on a scalable and reliable cloud infrastructure. Azure App Service is a web application, REST API, and mobile back end hosting service that runs on HTTP. You can code in any language you like, including.NET,.NET Core, Java, Ruby, Node.js, PHP, or Python. Both Windows and Linux-based systems make it easy to run and scale applications.

App Service not only adds the security, load balancing, autoscaling, and automated management features of Microsoft Azure to your application. It also has DevOps features including continuous deployment via Azure DevOps, GitHub, Docker Hub, and other sources, package management, staging environments, custom domains, and TLS/SSL certificates.

  1. Create an Azure account (Microsoft.azure.com)
  2. Sign in to your Microsoft Azure account (portal.azure.com)
  3. On your Microsoft Azure home tab, click on App Services

Creating a New App Service Plan

  1. Then click on create (+Create) on the top option bars
  2. Specify your choice of subscription
  3. Click on Create a new resource group

  1. In the name section under the Instance Details, add a unique name.
  2. In the publish section, select code if you will be publishing a code(code can be published using Visual studio), Docker container (packaged Softwares that have everything the software needs to run including libraries, system tools, code, and runtime), or Static web app(you can build a static web app on azure using Quickstart).
  3. Select your runtime stack in the runtime stack drop-down list. A runtime stack simply means the programming language used in building your app.
  4. Select your operating system, either Windows or Linux

  5. Select a region that best suits your app service plan

  6. Click on review and create, review your actions and create if all is set up.


    Hurray!! your App Service is all set up.

In my next blog, I will be sharing insights on how to build a static web app on Azure. I hope you are just as excited as I am.

Alexander Kammerer - Apr 3

item.title: 你的.js | yourid.js
item.description:
一个简单的 id 生成器。 使用起来快速、简单且有趣。

安装

用法

var yourId = require ( " yourid " ); // Generate a new id console . log ( yourId . generate ({ length : 10 , // the length of the random id string keyspace : " customkeyspace " , // the keyspace to use prefix : " yourPrefix " , // set a prefix includePrefix : true , // you can set it to FALSE or TRUE }) ); // => Random ID String length 10 & Prefix yourPrefix



原文:

A simple id generator. Thats fast, easy, and fun to use.

Installation

Usage 

var yourId = require("yourid"); // Generate a new id console.log( yourId.generate({ length: 10, // the length of the random id string keyspace: "customkeyspace", // the keyspace to use prefix: "yourPrefix", // set a prefix includePrefix: true, // you can set it to FALSE or TRUE })
); // => Random ID String length 10 & Prefix yourPrefix

item.title: 如何在 Laravel 9 中创建自定义服务提供者 | How to Create Custom Service Provider in Laravel 9
item.description:
原贴@https ://codeanddeploy.com访问并下载示例代码：
在这篇文章中，我将分享如何在 Laravel 9 中创建自定义服务提供者。 如果你有一个功能需要更改 Laravel 语言环境，或者可能根据你的应用程序设置更改时区。 然后我们使用服务提供商来执行此操作。

使用 Artisan 在 Laravel 中创建服务提供者

让我们运行命令php artisan make:provider AppConfigServiceProvider 。 您可以根据需要更改班级名称。

一旦完成，它将在app/Providers中生成一个文件AppConfigServiceProvider.php 。 这是下面生成的代码：

 <?php namespace App\Providers ; use Illuminate\Support\ServiceProvider ; class AppConfigServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider { /** * Register services. * * @return void */ public function register () { // } /** * Bootstrap services. * * @return void */ public function boot () { } }

如您所见，有两种方法register()和boot() 。

下面是示例代码：

 public function register () { $this -> app -> singleton ( any_class , function ( $app ) { return new AnyClass ( $app ); }); }

这将有助于将类或接口绑定到只应解析一次的容器中。 一旦解决了单例绑定，后续调用将返回相同的对象实例，而不是新实例。

这就是为什么在我的示例中，如果我们想从其原始配置更改 Laravel 语言环境和时区，我们需要在boot()方法中实现代码，以便我们可以访问所有已注册的服务。

这是我的例子。

 /** * Bootstrap services. * * @return void */ public function boot () { app () -> setLocale ( setting ( 'locale' ) ?? config ( 'app.locale' ) ); config ([ 'app.timezone' => setting ( 'timezone' ) ?? config ( 'app.timezone' ) ]); }

配置完自定义提供程序后，我们需要在config/app.php中注册它以启用它。

怎么做？ 首先搜索 providers 键或这个'providers' => [...]然后在该数组中添加命名空间或这个App\Providers\AppConfigServiceProvider::class, 。 请参见下面的示例：

 'providers' => [ ... /** * Setting Service Providers.. */ App\Providers\AppConfigServiceProvider :: class , ],

如您所见，我们根据应用程序设置更改区域设置和时区。 我希望它有帮助:)

注意：如果你想使用setting()函数，你需要安装akaunting/laravel-setting包。

我希望本教程可以帮助你。 如果您想下载此代码，请访问此处https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/how-to-create-custom-service-provider-in-laravel-9 。

快乐编码:)



原文:

Originally posted @ https://codeanddeploy.com visit and download the sample code:
https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/how-to-create-custom-service-provider-in-laravel-9

In this post, I will share how to create a custom service provider in Laravel 9. If you have a feature that needs to change the Laravel locale or maybe change the timezone base on your application settings. Then we are using a service provider to do this.

Create Service Provider in Laravel using Artisan

Let's run the command php artisan make:provider AppConfigServiceProvider. AppConfigServiceProvider is your service provider class name. You can change with your class name need.

Once done it will generate a file AppConfigServiceProvider.php inside app/Providers. Here is the code generated below:

<?php namespace App\Providers; use Illuminate\Support\ServiceProvider; class AppConfigServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{ /** * Register services. * * @return void */ public function register() { // } /** * Bootstrap services. * * @return void */ public function boot() { }
}

As you can see there are two methods register() and boot().

The register() method

The register() method in the Service Provider allows us to define bindings to our service container. Here is the sample code below:

public function register()
{ $this->app->singleton(any_class, function ($app) { return new AnyClass($app); });
}

The $this->app is a global variable that is available inside Laravel which use to access the internal settings of the application.

The singleton is a trait that is taken from the Ruby module. It will help to bind a class or interface into the container that should only be resolved one time. Once a singleton binding is resolved, the same object instance will be returned on subsequent calls instead of a new instance.

The boot() method

The boot() method is used to access all the services which are registered in register() method. That's why in my example if we want to change the Laravel locale and timezone from its original configuration we need to implement the code inside the boot() method so that we can access all the registered services.

Here is my example to do it.

/** * Bootstrap services. * * @return void */
public function boot()
{ app()->setLocale( setting('locale') ?? config('app.locale') ); config([ 'app.timezone' => setting('timezone') ?? config('app.timezone') ]);
}

Once done configuring your Custom Provider we need to register it inside config/app.php to enable it.

How to do it? First search the providers key or this 'providers' => [...] then inside that array add the namespace or this App\Providers\AppConfigServiceProvider::class,. See the example below:

'providers' => [ ... /** * Setting Service Providers.. */ App\Providers\AppConfigServiceProvider::class,
],

As you can see we change the locale and timezone based on our application settings. I hope it helps :)

NOTE: If you want to use the setting() function you need to install akaunting/laravel-setting package.

I hope this tutorial can help you. Kindly visit here https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/how-to-create-custom-service-provider-in-laravel-9 if you want to download this code.

Happy coding :)
item.author: codeanddeploy

item.title: 玩弄 Kotlin 的上下文接收器 | Toying with Kotlin's context receivers
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我想玩弄它们以了解它们的用处。

请注意，如果您想一起玩，您需要使用-Xcontext-receivers标志进行编译。

上下文接收器背后的主要思想是将附加参数传递给函数，而无需显式执行。

简化的模型样本

让我们从一个简单的例子开始来展示它是如何工作的。 我们想模拟两个银行账户之间的简单转账操作。 账户余额存储在数据库中，相应的贷记/还款操作必须是交易性的。

让我们关注AccountService.transfer()函数。 它需要一个包含多个操作的Transaction实例：

 class AccountService { fun transfer ( tx : Transaction , vararg operations : () -> Unit ) { tx . start () try { operations . forEach { it . invoke () } tx . commit () } catch ( e : Exception ) { tx . rollback () } } }

我们可以将上面的代码称为：

 val service = AccountService () val transaction = Transaction () val repo = AccountRepo () service . transfer ( transaction , { repo . credit ( account1 , 10.5 ) }, { repo . debit ( account2 , 10.5 ) } )

使用扩展函数改进代码

我们可以利用扩展函数对上面的代码稍加改进。 我们可以将后者迁移到扩展函数，而不是将Transaction定义为transfer()函数的参数。

 class AccountService { fun Transaction . transfer ( vararg operations : () -> Unit ) { start () // 1 try { operations . forEach { it . invoke () } commit () // 1 } catch ( e : Exception ) { rollback () // 1 } } }
  1. 隐式this引用Transaction对象

在AccountService的上下文中，我们现在可以在现有Transaction上调用transfer()函数。

 with ( service ) { // 1 transaction . transfer ( // 2 { repo . credit ( account1 , 10.5 ) }, { repo . debit ( account2 , 10.5 ) } ) }
  1. 将service实例纳入范围
  2. 所以在transaction对象上调用transfer是有效的

可以从两个不同的角度分析新的调用代码：

  • 输入的原始字符数更少 - 更简洁
  • 但是，语义完全不同。 新代码意味着在AccountService的上下文中，我们可以在现有的Transaction对象上调用transfer() 。

我认为语义是错误的； 它应该是相反的。 它应该是相反的。 在Transaction的上下文中，我们应该能够在现有的AccountService对象上调用transfer() ：

 with ( transaction ) { service . transfer ( { repo . credit ( account1 , 10.5 ) }, { repo . debit ( account2 , 10.5 ) } ) }

恕我直言，与错误语义的成本相比，简洁的价值很小。 不幸的是，对于当前的语言结构，修复语义意味着我们需要将transfer()函数移至Transaction 。 这将是糟糕的建模，因为传输是服务的责任。

上下文接收器救援

正如我在介绍中提到的，上下文接收器背后的想法是以某种方式“传递”函数参数，而不是明确地说明它们。

 context ( Foo , Bar , Baz ) fun myfunction () {}

要调用这样的函数，需要将每个上下文类型的对象带入“范围”。 我们可以通过with函数来实现：

 val foo = Foo () val bar = Bar () val baz = Baz () with ( foo ) { // 1 with ( bar ) { // 2 with ( baz ) { // 3 myfunction () // 4 } } }
  1. 将foo纳入范围
  2. 将bar纳入范围
  3. 将baz纳入范围
  4. 调用函数

虽然上面的代码可以编译，但它只有在我们使用上下文对象时才适用。 调用语法与带有接收器的 lambda 之一相同：

 context ( Foo , Bar , Baz ) fun myfunction () { println ( this @Foo ) println ( this @Bar ) println ( this @Baz ) }

我们使用上下文接收器能够使用想要的代码编写代码：

 class AccountService { context ( Transaction ) fun transfer ( vararg operations : () -> Unit ) { start () // 1 try { operations . forEach { it . invoke () } commit () // 1 } catch ( e : Exception ) { rollback () // 1 } } }
  1. 隐式this引用Transaction对象。 我们不需要进一步限定类名，因为没有其他上下文对象

我们现在可以使用正确的语义相应地调用代码：

 with ( transaction ) { // 1 service . transfer ( // 2 { repo . credit ( account1 , 10.5 ) }, { repo . debit ( account2 , 10.5 ) } ) }
  1. 将transaction纳入范围
  2. 在范围内使用transaction对象

讨论

上下文接收器允许我们使用正确的调用代码语义来实现 API。 没有他们是不可能的。

我在 Scala 中只涉足了一点，但我总是发现 Scala 2 的implicit实现很差。 要将对象引入范围，您只需要在文件顶部进行import ，这可能与使用它的位置相距甚远。 它使理解代码变得更加困难，并增加了维护成本。 我相信 Kotlin 的实现要理智得多。 您可以使用with在上下文接收器中实现范围界定，这会将上下文靠近调用站点并使用代码块发出信号。

然而，并不是所有的独角兽和彩虹。 特别是，我有点担心上下文接收器会被滥用。 当然，每种语言的每个新功能通常都是这种情况。 然而，我觉得这个滥用的可能性是巨大的。 只有未来会告诉我们。

与此同时，我很想看到上下文接收器的更多不同用法以及它们可以解锁哪些模式。

更进一步：

最初于 2022 年 5 月 14日发表于A Java Geek



原文:

Kotlin added the idea of Context Receivers in version 1.6.20. In this post, I'd like to toy with them to understand how useful they can be.

Note that if you want to play along, you'll need to compile with the -Xcontext-receivers flag.

The main idea behind context receivers is to pass additional parameters to a function without having to do it explicitly.

A simplified model sample

Let's start with a simple example to show how it works. We want to model a simple transfer operation between two bank accounts. Accounts balance is stored in a database, and respective credit/debut operations must be transactional.

Let's focus on the AccountService.transfer() function. It requires a Transaction instance that wraps several of operations:

class AccountService { fun transfer(tx: Transaction, vararg operations: () -> Unit) { tx.start() try { operations.forEach { it.invoke() } tx.commit() } catch (e: Exception) { tx.rollback() } }
}

We can call the above code as:

val service = AccountService()
val transaction = Transaction()
val repo = AccountRepo()
service.transfer( transaction, { repo.credit(account1, 10.5) }, { repo.debit(account2, 10.5) }
)

Improving the code with extension functions

We can slightly improve the above code by making use of extension functions. Instead of defining the Transaction as a parameter to the transfer() function, we can migrate the latter to an extension function.

class AccountService { fun Transaction.transfer(vararg operations: () -> Unit) { start() // 1 try { operations.forEach { it.invoke() } commit() // 1 } catch (e: Exception) { rollback() // 1 } }
}
  1. Implicit this references the Transaction object

Within the context of an AccountService, we can now call the transfer() function on an existing Transaction.

with(service) { // 1 transaction.transfer( // 2 { repo.credit(account1, 10.5) }, { repo.debit(account2, 10.5) } )
}
  1. Bring the service instance in scope
  2. So it's valid to call transfer on the transaction object

One can analyze the new calling code from two different viewpoints:

  • The raw number of characters typed is less - it's more concise
  • The semantics is radically different, though. The new code means that in the context of an AccountService, we can call transfer() on an existing Transaction object.

I think the semantics is wrong; it should be the opposite. it should be the opposite. In the context of Transaction, we should be able to call transfer() on an existing AccountService object:

with(transaction) { service.transfer( { repo.credit(account1, 10.5) }, { repo.debit(account2, 10.5) } )
}

IMHO, conciseness has very little value compared to the cost of wrong semantics. Unfortunately, with the current language constructs, fixing semantics means we would need to move the transfer() function to Transaction. It would be lousy modeling as the transfer is the responsibility of the service.

Context receivers to the rescue

As I mentioned in the introduction, the idea behind context receivers is to somehow "pass" function parameters without being explicit about them.

context(Foo, Bar, Baz)
fun myfunction() {}

To call such a function, one needs to bring an object of each contextual type "in scope". We can achieve it with the with function:

val foo = Foo()
val bar = Bar()
val baz = Baz()
with(foo) { // 1 with(bar) { // 2 with (baz) { // 3 myfunction() // 4 } }
}
  1. Bring foo in scope
  2. Bring bar in scope
  3. Bring baz in scope
  4. Call the function

While the code above compiles, it's only applicable if we use the contextual objects. The calling syntax is the same as the one of lambdas with receiver:

context(Foo, Bar, Baz)
fun myfunction() { println(this@Foo) println(this@Bar) println(this@Baz)
}

We use context receivers to be able to write code using the wanted code:

class AccountService { context(Transaction) fun transfer(vararg operations: () -> Unit) { start() // 1 try { operations.forEach { it.invoke() } commit() // 1 } catch (e: Exception) { rollback() // 1 } }
}
  1. Implicit this references the Transaction object. We don't need to qualify further with the class name as there's no other context object

We can now call the code accordingly, with the correct semantics:

with(transaction) { // 1 service.transfer( // 2 { repo.credit(account1, 10.5) }, { repo.debit(account2, 10.5) } )
}
  1. Bring transaction in scope
  2. Use the transaction object in scope

Discussion

Context receivers allow us to implement the API with the correct calling code semantics. It's not possible without them.

I've dabbled only a bit in Scala, but I've always found Scala 2's implicit poorly implemented. To bring an object in scope, you only need an import at the top of the file, which might be very far from where it's used. It makes understanding the code much harder and increases maintenance costs. Scala 3 has made the implicitness much more explicit and fixed some of my grievances.

I believe that Kotlin's implementation is much saner. You achieve scoping in context receivers with with, which brings the context close to the call site and signals it with a code block.

However, it's not all unicorns and rainbows. In particular, I'm a bit worried that context receivers will be abused. Granted, it's generally the case for every new feature for every language. Yet, I feel the potential for abuse is enormous with this one. Only the future will tell.

In the meantime, I'm curious to see more different usages of context receivers and what patterns they can unlock.

To go further:

Originally published at A Java Geek on May 14 th, 2022
item.author: nfrankel

item.title: HTML 段落 #7 | HTML Paragraphs #7
item.description:
段落总是从新行开始，通常是一段文本。

段落总是从新行开始，浏览器会在段落前后自动添加一些空白（边距）。

例子

<p>This is a paragraph.</p> <p>This is another paragraph.</p>

您无法确定 HTML 将如何显示。

大屏幕或小屏幕，以及调整大小的窗口会产生不同的结果。

使用 HTML，您无法通过在 HTML 代码中添加额外的空格或额外的行来更改显示。

显示页面时，浏览器会自动删除任何多余的空格和行：

例子

<p> This paragraph contains a lot of lines in the source code, but the browser ignores it. </p> <p> This paragraph contains a lot of spaces in the source code, but the browser ignores it. </p>

例子

<h1>This is heading 1</h1> <p>This is some text.</p> <hr> <h2>This is heading 2</h2> <p>This is some other text.</p> <hr>

如果您想要换行（换行）而不开始新段落，请使用<br> ：

例子

<p>This is<br>a paragraph<br>with line breaks.</p>

诗歌问题

这首诗将显示在一行中：

例子

<p> My Bonnie lies over the ocean. My Bonnie lies over the sea. My Bonnie lies over the ocean. Oh, bring back my Bonnie to me. </p>

解决方案 - HTML
 元素

例子

<pre> My Bonnie lies over the ocean. My Bonnie lies over the sea. My Bonnie lies over the ocean. Oh, bring back my Bonnie to me. </pre>


原文:

A paragraph always starts on a new line, and is usually a block of text.

HTML Paragraphs

The HTML <p> element defines a paragraph.

A paragraph always starts on a new line, and browsers automatically add some white space (a margin) before and after a paragraph.

Example 

<p>This is a paragraph.</p>
<p>This is another paragraph.</p>

HTML Display

You cannot be sure how HTML will be displayed.

Large or small screens, and resized windows will create different results.

With HTML, you cannot change the display by adding extra spaces or extra lines in your HTML code.

The browser will automatically remove any extra spaces and lines when the page is displayed:

Example 

<p>
This paragraph
contains a lot of lines
in the source code,
but the browser
ignores it.
</p>

<p>
This paragraph
contains         a lot of spaces
in the source         code,
but the        browser
ignores it.
</p>

HTML Horizontal Rules

The <hr> tag defines a thematic break in an HTML page, and is most often displayed as a horizontal rule.

The <hr> element is used to separate content (or define a change) in an HTML page:

Example 

<h1>This is heading 1</h1>
<p>This is some text.</p>
<hr>
<h2>This is heading 2</h2>
<p>This is some other text.</p>
<hr>

The <hr> tag is an empty tag, which means that it has no end tag.

HTML Line Breaks

The HTML <br> element defines a line break.

Use <br> if you want a line break (a new line) without starting a new paragraph:

Example 

<p>This is<br>a paragraph<br>with line breaks.</p>

The <br> tag is an empty tag, which means that it has no end tag.

The Poem Problem

This poem will display on a single line:

Example 

<p>
  My Bonnie lies over the ocean.

  My Bonnie lies over the sea.

  My Bonnie lies over the ocean.

  Oh, bring back my Bonnie to me.
</p>

Solution - The HTML 
 Element

The HTML <pre> element defines preformatted text.

The text inside a <pre> element is displayed in a fixed-width font (usually Courier), and it preserves both spaces and line breaks:

Example 

<pre>
  My Bonnie lies over the ocean.

  My Bonnie lies over the sea.

  My Bonnie lies over the ocean.

  Oh, bring back my Bonnie to me.
</pre>
item.author: goodboyks

item.title: Sveltekit 简介 | Introduction to Sveltekit
item.description:
它是一个前端 javascript 库，具有以下功能 -

  • 服务器端和客户端渲染
  • 带有路由系统
  • 提供后端 API
  • 代码拆分

这也是一个流行的 SPA 框架。 根据 mdn 网络文档

开始构建 SvelteKit 应用程序的最简单方法是运行

为了拥有一个正常运行的苗条应用程序，您需要确保您的系统安装了更新版本的node js 。 如果你使用 vs code 扩展，svelte for vs code 是一个很好的安装扩展。 这将为我们提供 svelte 组件的语法高亮显示。

在此处了解有关苗条套件的更多信息



原文:

Svelte-kit is a framework for building website with Svelte. It is a front end javascript library with the following features -

  • Server and client side rendering
  • comes with routing systems
  • provides a backend API
  • code splitting

This is also a popular SPA framework. According to mdn web docs

An SPA (Single-page application) is a web app implementation that loads only a single web document, and then updates the body content of that single document via JavaScript APIs such as XMLHttpRequest and Fetch when different content is to be shown.

The easiest way to start building a SvelteKit app is to run

In order to have a proper functioning svelte app you need to make sure you have an updated version of node js installed to your system. Svelte sometimes runs into problems with older versions of node.

If you use vs code extensions svelte for vs code is a good extension to install. This will provide us with syntax highlighting for svelte components.

Learn more about svelte kit here

item.title: Laravel 最简单的实现自定义过期记住我登录 | Easiest Implementation Custom Expiration for Laravel Remember Me Login
item.description:
原贴@https ://codeanddeploy.com访问并下载示例代码：
在我之前的帖子中，我分享了如何为您的Laravel Remember Me身份验证/登录实现自定义过期。 但是当我测试我的代码时，如果我删除会话似乎不起作用，它将被重定向到登录页面。 所以我进行了研究，但仍然没有运气，然后我检查了 Auth Session Guard，我发现有一种公共方法可以将记住我的持续时间设置为分钟。

这是我的LoginController的完整代码。

 <?php namespace App\Http\Controllers ; use Illuminate\Http\Request ; use App\Http\Requests\LoginRequest ; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Auth ; class LoginController extends Controller { /** * Display login page. * * @return Renderable */ public function show () { return view ( 'auth.login' ); } /** * Handle account login request * * @param LoginRequest $request * * @return \Illuminate\Http\Response */ public function login ( LoginRequest $request ) { $credentials = $request -> getCredentials (); if ( ! Auth :: validate ( $credentials )) : return redirect () -> to ( 'login' ) -> withErrors ( trans ( 'auth.failed' )); endif ; $user = Auth :: getProvider () -> retrieveByCredentials ( $credentials ); if ( $request -> get ( 'remember' )) : Auth :: setRememberDuration ( 43200 ); // equivalent to 1 month endif ; Auth :: login ( $user , $request -> get ( 'remember' )); return $this -> authenticated ( $request , $user ); } /** * Handle response after user authenticated * * @param Request $request * @param Auth $user * * @return \Illuminate\Http\Response */ protected function authenticated ( Request $request , $user ) { return redirect () -> intended (); } }

在我之前的教程中，我创建了一个自定义的 Trait 类，用于自定义 Laravel 记住我的身份验证/登录。 但现在我删除了它，只是将 3 行代码放在Auth::login()方法之前。

 if ( $request -> get ( 'remember' )) : Auth :: setRememberDuration ( 43200 ); endif ;

现在只需 3 行代码，你就可以自定义 Laravel 记住我了。 我希望本教程可以帮助你。 如果您想下载此代码，请访问这里https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/easyest-implementation-custom-expiration-for-laravel-remember-me-login 。

快乐编码:)



原文:

Originally posted @ https://codeanddeploy.com visit and download the sample code:
https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/easiest-implementation-custom-expiration-for-laravel-remember-me-login

In my previous post, I share how to implement custom expiration for your Laravel Remember Me authentication/login. But when I test my code it seems not to work if I delete the session and it will be redirected me to the login page. So I researched but still no luck then I check the Auth Session Guard and I found that there is a public method that can set the Remember me duration by minutes.

Here is the full code of my LoginController.

<?php namespace App\Http\Controllers; use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use App\Http\Requests\LoginRequest;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Auth; class LoginController extends Controller
{ /** * Display login page. * * @return Renderable */ public function show() { return view('auth.login'); } /** * Handle account login request * * @param LoginRequest $request * * @return \Illuminate\Http\Response */ public function login(LoginRequest $request) { $credentials = $request->getCredentials(); if(!Auth::validate($credentials)): return redirect()->to('login') ->withErrors(trans('auth.failed')); endif; $user = Auth::getProvider()->retrieveByCredentials($credentials); if($request->get('remember')): Auth::setRememberDuration(43200); // equivalent to 1 month endif; Auth::login($user, $request->get('remember')); return $this->authenticated($request, $user); } /** * Handle response after user authenticated * * @param Request $request * @param Auth $user * * @return \Illuminate\Http\Response */ protected function authenticated(Request $request, $user) { return redirect()->intended(); }
}

In my previous tutorial, I created a custom Trait Class that customizes the Laravel remember me authentication/login. But now I removed it and just put the 3 lines of code before Auth::login() method.

if($request->get('remember')): Auth::setRememberDuration(43200);
endif;

In just 3 lines you will now customize the Laravel remember me. I hope this tutorial can help you. Kindly visit here https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/easiest-implementation-custom-expiration-for-laravel-remember-me-login if you want to download this code.

Happy coding :)
item.author: codeanddeploy

item.title: Azure 应用服务 | Azure App Service
item.description:
它们对开发人员非常有用，因为它提供了所有运行 Azure Functions 的 Web 应用程序、API 应用程序、移动应用程序和函数应用程序。 它们提供了许多令人惊叹的功能，例如简单的身份验证、移动应用程序的离线同步、混合连接等。

它是一种提供多种服务的平台即服务 (PaaS)。 它与 Windows 和 Linux 兼容，可以通过 Azure 门户进行自定义。

对于无缝云安装，它支持安全性和合规性，包括服务组织控制 (SOC) 和支付卡行业 (PCI)。

  • 与 Git、GitHub、GitHub Actions、Docker Hub、Bitbucket 和 Azure 容器注册表集成以支持 CI/CD。

  • 它为安全地大规模运行应用服务应用程序提供了一个完全隔离的环境。

  • 支持连接到本地和 Azure 资源。

  • 它通过与 Application Insights、Azure Monitor 和应用服务日志集成，提供有关 CPU、内存利用率、错误模式和性能监控的详细见解。

  • 支持应用程序和 HTTP 日志的 Azure 存储容器备份和日志流。

  • 备份和日志流：支持自动备份到 Azure 存储容器，以及将应用程序和 HTTP 日志的日志流自动备份到存储容器中。

    • 部署槽

    部署槽是与主 Web 应用一起运行的功能齐全的应用服务。 此功能可用于测试和部署应用程序的新版本。 它允许您将部署槽切换到新的生产版本而无需停机。

    部署中心

    此功能允许您将代码存储库连接到您的 Web 应用程序，并在更新时生成和部署该代码，这在 CI/CD 集成中非常有用。

    授权和认证

    借助应用服务，您可以轻松地对用户进行身份验证，然后才能使用您的应用。 这可以通过简单地选择使用哪个身份提供者来完成。 通过添加 SSL 证书并将它们绑定到分配给您的应用服务的域，您可以保护应用服务的流量。 在其他地方购买的 SSL 证书可用于您的首选域。 您还可以使用 Azure 门户购买 Azure 应用服务证书。

    还有一些其他功能将在以后的帖子中讨论。

    谢谢阅读！

    卡尔·马蒂亚斯·莫伯格 - May 9 5月9日



    原文:

    Azure App Service is a fully managed computing platform for hosting websites and web apps. They are extremely useful for developers because it offers Web Apps, API Apps, Mobile Apps and Function Apps that all runs the Azure Functions. They provide a host of amazing features like easy authentication, offline sync for Mobile Apps, hybrid connections etc.

    It is a platform-as-a-service (PaaS) that provides many services. Microsoft Azure allows you to concentrate on your business logic while Azure handles the infrastructure required to launch and scale your apps. It's compatible with both Windows and Linux which can be customized through the Azure portal.

    Benefits of Azure App Services

    • Web Apps can be written in a variety of programming languages and frameworks, including Node.js, Java, Ruby, Python, and PHP, and they can operate on Linux, Windows, and Docker Containers.

    • For seamless cloud installations, it enables Security and Compliance, including Service Organisation Controls (SOC) and Payment Card Industry (PCI).

    • Integrates with Git, GitHub, GitHub Actions, Docker Hub, Bitbucket, and Azure Container Registry to support CI/CD.

    • It provides a fully isolated environment for securely running App Service apps at scale.

    • Support for connecting to both on-premises and Azure resources.

    • It provides detailed insights into CPU, Memory Utilization, Error patterns, and performance monitoring by integrating with Application Insights, Azure Monitor, and App service logs.

    • Supports Azure Storage Container backups and log streams of both application and HTTP logs.

    • Backups and Log Streams:  Supports Automated Backups into Azure Storage Container and Log Streams of Both Application and HTTP logs into Storage Containers.

    Azure Service Shared Features

    Deployment Slots

    A deployment slot is a fully functional App Service that runs alongside your main Web App. This feature can be used to test and deploy a new version of your application. It allows you to switch a deployment slot into a new production build with no downtime.

    Deployment Center

    This feature allows you to connect a code repository to your Web App and have that code produced and deployed anytime it is updated which is very usefulin CI/CD integrations.

    Authorization and Authentication

    With App Service you can easily authenticate your users before they can use your app. It can be done by simply selecting which identity provider to use. Google, Facebook, Twitter, Microsoft, Azure Active Directory, or a custom provider are all options.

    SSL Configurations

    By adding SSL certificates and binding them to domains allocated to your App Service, you may secure traffic to your App Service. Azure App Service URL has an SSL certificate installed by default. SSL certificates purchased elsewhere can be used for your preferred domains. You can also purchase an Azure App Service Certificate using the Azure portal.

    There are a couple of other features which will be discussed in later posts.

    Thanks for reading!

    Karl Mathias Moberg - May 9
item.author: annysah

item.title: Laravel 重定向备忘单 | Laravel Redirect Cheat Sheet
item.description:
原贴@https ://codeanddeploy.com访问并下载示例代码：
在这篇文章中，您将学习一些对您的 Laravel 开发有用的Laravel 重定向备忘单。

它将重定向到另一个页面，状态 302 表示临时重定向。

 //redirect to another page. Status is 302 indicating temporay redirect Route :: redirect ( '/page' , '/new-page' );

此示例可以将页面状态定义为指示永久重定向的第三个参数。

 //can pass status as 3rd Parameter. 301 indicates permanent redirect Route :: redirect ( '/page' , '/new-page' , 301 );

与上例相同，但无需设置 301 页面状态

//Same as above Route :: permanentRedirect ( '/page' , '/new-page' );

这个例子表明我们可以使用参数重定向。

 //Redirect along with the parameter Route :: redirect ( '/page/{param}' , '/new-page/{param}' );

此示例可以使用所有可用参数进行重定向。

 //Redirect with all parameters Route :: redirect ( '/page/{params?}' , '/new-page/{params?}' ) -> where ( 'params' , '(.*)' );

我希望本教程可以帮助你。 如果您想下载此代码，请访问这里https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/laravel-redirect-cheat-sheet 。

快乐编码:)



原文:

Originally posted @ https://codeanddeploy.com visit and download the sample code:
https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/laravel-redirect-cheat-sheet

In this post, you will learn some Laravel Redirect Cheat Sheet that is useful in your Laravel development.

It will redirect to another page with status 302 indicating a temporary redirect.

//redirect to another page. Status is 302 indicating temporay redirect
Route::redirect('/page', '/new-page');

This example can define page status as 3rd parameter which indicates a permanent redirect.

//can pass status as 3rd Parameter. 301 indicates permanent redirect
Route::redirect('/page', '/new-page', 301);

Same as above example but no need to set a 301-page status

//Same as above
Route::permanentRedirect('/page', '/new-page');

This example shows that we can redirect with parameters.

//Redirect along with the parameter
Route::redirect('/page/{param}', '/new-page/{param}');

This example can redirect with all parameters available.

//Redirect with all parameters
Route::redirect('/page/{params?}', '/new-page/{params?}')->where('params', '(.*)');

I hope this tutorial can help you. Kindly visit here https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/laravel-redirect-cheat-sheet if you want to download this code.

Happy coding :)
item.author: codeanddeploy

item.title: 人类可读的 PHP Carbon 格式 | PHP Carbon Formatting that Readable for Humans
item.description:
原贴@https ://codeanddeploy.com访问并下载示例代码：
在此示例中，我将向您展示如何在 PHP 中使用人类可读的碳格式化日期的示例。

请参见下面的示例：

迄今为止的 PHP Carbon 格式示例

<?php $date = Carbon :: now (); echo $date -> toDateString (); //Result: 2022-05-06

几天前的 PHP 碳格式

<?php echo Carbon :: now () -> subDays ( 3 ) -> diffForHumans (); //Result: 3 days ago

数周前 PHP Carbon 格式

<?php echo Carbon :: now () -> subWeek ( 3 ) -> diffForHumans (); //Result: 3 weeks ago

我希望本教程可以帮助你。 如果您想下载此代码，请访问此处https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/php/php-carbon-formatting-that-readable-for-humans 。

快乐编码:)



原文:

Originally posted @ https://codeanddeploy.com visit and download the sample code:
https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/php/php-carbon-formatting-that-readable-for-humans

In this example, I will show you an example of how to use Carbon Formatting dates in PHP that are readable for humans.

See the example below:

PHP Carbon Format to Date Example 

<?php $date = Carbon::now(); echo $date->toDateString();
//Result: 2022-05-06

PHP Carbon Format to Date & Time Example 

<?php $date = Carbon::now(); echo $date->toDateTimeString();
//Result: 2022-05-06 07:20:48

PHP Carbon Formatted Example 

<?php $date = Carbon::now(); echo $date->toFormattedDateString();
//Result: May 6, 2022

PHP Carbon Format to Time Example 

<?php $date = Carbon::now(); echo $date->toTimeString();
//Result: 07:24:08

PHP Carbon Format to Day, Date, and Time 

<?php $date = Carbon::now(); echo $date->toDayDateTimeString();
//Result: Fri, May 6, 2022 7:25 AM

PHP Carbon Format Days Ago 

<?php echo Carbon::now()->subDays(3)->diffForHumans();
//Result: 3 days ago

PHP Carbon Format Weeks Ago 

<?php echo Carbon::now()->subWeek(3)->diffForHumans();
//Result: 3 weeks ago

PHP Carbon Format Months Ago 

<?php echo Carbon::now()->subMonth(3)->diffForHumans();
//Result: 3 months ago

PHP Carbon Format Long Relative for Humans 

<?php echo Carbon::create('2022')->longRelativeDiffForHumans('2019');
//Result: 4 months 5 days 7 hours 31 minutes 21 seconds ago

I hope this tutorial can help you. Kindly visit here https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/php/php-carbon-formatting-that-readable-for-humans if you want to download this code.

Happy coding :)
item.author: codeanddeploy

item.title: 在优化您的 Lighthouse 分数之前执行此操作 | Do this before optimizing your Lighthouse score
item.description:
它根据性能、可访问性、最佳实践和 SEO 为您提供最高 100 分。

真的那么重要吗？

很容易陷入优化您的 Lighthouse 分数并试图达到 100 的陷阱。对您的代码进行小的调整并看到该数字上升并变成绿色是令人满意的。

优化我们可以量化的内容更容易。 这就是为什么我们用薪水和净资产来衡量职业成功，而不是工作满意度和自由度。 学校和某些人（🙋）对成绩的痴迷也是如此。

如果 Lighthouse 告诉您您的网站存在一些重大问题，请继续进行修复。 但要专注于目标。 在某个时刻，您可以为您的网站做的其他事情比将您的分数从 90 分提高到 100 分具有更大的影响。

成功网站的灯塔分数

过度设计会让你从最重要的事情上分心。 让我们来看看一些著名的网站和他们的灯塔分数。

与其优化你的 Lighthouse 分数，不如问问自己这些问题。

您的站点在所有断点中都运行良好吗？

我们通常首先为桌面设计。 很有可能，您的移动用户比例很高，因为我们都在使用手机。 这。 时间。 因此，不要将您的移动体验视为事后的想法。 它甚至可能比桌面更重要。

你的网站的目标是什么，它的设计方式是否能让你的用户做你想做的事？

这称为号召性用语 (CTA)。 无论是投资组合网站、登录页面、电子商务还是 SaaS，您都应该知道您希望用户在离开之前做什么，例如：

  • 对于投资组合网站，您希望潜在客户与您联系并讨论潜在的工作。
  • 对于 SaaS 登录页面，您希望用户注册免费试用。
  • 对于博客，您希望用户订阅您的时事通讯。

通过以下方式使您的 CTA 响亮而清晰：

  • 让您的 CTA 按钮脱颖而出。
  • 撰写文案，引导用户点击这些 CTA。
  • 底部有一个 CTA 部分，用户在阅读并理解您提供的内容后会看到该部分。

我的页面在社交媒体上共享时是什么样的？

你写了一篇很棒的博客文章。 内容绝对🔥。 这将为您的读者带来很多价值。 然后你在 Twitter 上分享链接，预览看起来不像你试图告诉所有人的博客文章。 确保您拥有正确的元图像、标题和预览。 在 Facebook 和 Twitter 上测试它们。

您可以做些什么来让用户点击整个网站并转到不同的页面？

一旦您让某人访问您网站上的某个页面，请利用它并让他们轻松导航到其他页面。 这里有一些例子：

在每篇博文中，谈谈你自己并添加相关链接以进一步了解你。

对于产品、项目或博客文章，显示类似以下内容的相关内容：“您可能会喜欢这个……”

面包屑返回到层次结构的页面。

用于对您的站点进行高级概述的超级菜单。

概括

这很好，但不要陷入过度设计的困境。 想想你可以从事哪些其他具有更大影响的任务。

这篇文章最初发表在trishalim.com上。



原文:

Lighthouse is a tool for measuring page quality that gives you tips and recommendations in improving user experience. It gives you a score of up to 100 based on performance, accessibility, best practices and SEO.

Is it really that important?

It's easy to fall into the trap of optimizing your Lighthouse score and trying to reach 100. It's satisfying to make small tweaks to your code and see that number go up and turn green.

It's easier to optimize what we can quantify. That's why we measure career success with salary and net worth, rather than job satisfaction and freedom. It's the same for school and some people's (🙋) obsession with grades.

If Lighthouse tells you that you have some major issues on your site, sure, go ahead and fix it. But focus on the goal. At a certain point, there are other things you can do for your site that have much higher impact than getting your score from 90 to 100.

Lighthouse scores of successful websites

Overengineering distracts you from the most important things. Let's go over some famous websites and their Lighthouse scores.

1. RemoteOK - a directory of remote jobs, making over $2M/year

2. Upwork - the world's leading marketplace for freelancers

3. Web3 Career - the #1 result when you search "web3 jobs" on Google

Instead of optimizing your Lighthouse score, ask yourself these questions.

Does your site work well in all breakpoints?

We usually design for desktop first. Chances are, you have a high percentage of mobile users, as we're on our phones all. the. time. So don't treat your mobile experience as an after thought. It might even be more important than desktop.

What's the goal of your website, and is it designed in a way that makes your users do what you want?

This is called a call-to-action (CTA). Whether it's a portfolio site, landing page, e-commerce or SaaS, you should know what you want your users to do before leaving, like:

  • For a portfolio website, you want leads to contact you and discuss potential work.
  • For a SaaS landing page, you want users to sign up for a free trial.
  • For a blog, you want users to subscribe to your newsletter.

Make your CTA loud and clear by:

  • Making your CTA buttons stand out.
  • Writing copy that leads users to click those CTA's.
  • Having a CTA section at the bottom, which users see after they've read and understood what you're offering.

What do my pages look like when shared on social media?

You've written an awesome blog post. The content is absolutely 🔥. This is gonna bring a lot of value to your readers. Then you share the link on Twitter and the preview looks nothing like the blog post you were trying to tell everyone about. 👎

Make sure you have your meta image, title and preview right. Test them on Facebook and Twitter.

What can you do to make users click throughout your site and go to different pages?

Once you get somebody to a page on your site, take advantage of it and make it easy for them to navigate to other pages. Here are a few examples:

On each blog post, talk a bit about yourself and add relevant links to get to know you more.

For products or projects or blog posts, show related content that says something along the lines of: "You might like this.."

Breadcrumbs to go back to pages up the hierarchy.

Mega menu for a high level overview of your site.

Summary

Lighthouse is a great tool to detect problems with your site, and they tell you what you can do to fix those problems. That's great, but don't get stuck overengineering. Think about what other tasks you can work on that have higher impact.

This post was originally published on trishalim.com.
item.author: trishathecookie

item.title: Jquery Checkbox 为一个角色分配多个权限 | Jquery Checkbox Assign Multiple Permissions to a Role
item.description:
原贴@https ://codeanddeploy.com访问并下载示例代码：
在这篇文章中，我将分享一个关于如何实现一个 jquery 复选框来为一个角色分配多个权限的简单代码。 如果您通过一个角色的复选框来实现多个权限，那么这篇文章可能会对您有所帮助。

之前，我发布了关于如何实现我创建的 jQuery TableCheckAll插件。 你可以在这里访问以前的帖子。

好的，您已经知道预期的输出是什么。 让我们来处理如何做到这一点。

首先，克隆 TableCheckAll jquery 插件。

 git clone https :// github . com / codeanddeploy / Jquery - Table - Check - All - Plugin . git

下载后，在您下载的文件夹中创建一个新文件。 假设index.html然后粘贴下面的代码：

二、这里是完整的源代码

在本节中，我们有一个可以在您的项目中实现的示例源代码。

注意： .select-all-permissions和.select-permission类能够选择所有模块权限。 所以你不需要替换这些类。

对于.select-permission类，您需要在每个模块权限复选框中添加此选项，包括模块选择所有权限复选框。 正如您在这段代码中看到的：

 <tr> <th scope= "row" > Clients </th> <td><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-all-module-permissions select-all-client-permissions" data-class= "select-all-client-permissions" > Select All </td> <td><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-module-permission select-client-permission" data-class= "select-client-permission" > Create Client <input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-module-permission select-client-permission" data-class= "select-client-permission" > Edit Client </td> </tr>

要触发模块权限，我们还需要使用自定义属性data-class="select-{module_name}-permission"和类名为每个模块权限添加类.select-module-permission和特定类。 请注意{module_name}是您的模块名称，没有空格。

然后在每个模块中选择所有复选框。 您需要添加.select-all-module-permissions类名。 因为我们使用它来循环所有可用的模块。

然后接下来我们将添加一个自定义属性data-class="select-all-{module_name}-permissions"并在每个模块中添加一个类.select-all-{module_name}-permissions选择所有复选框。

这是完整的源代码。

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <meta charset= "utf-8" /> <meta http-equiv= "X-UA-Compatible" content= "IE=edge" > <title> Jquery Checkbox Assign Multiple Permissions to a Role - codeanddeploy.com </title> <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1" > <link rel= "stylesheet" href= "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.0.2/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css" > <script src= "https://ajax.googleapis.com/ajax/libs/jquery/3.5.1/jquery.min.js" ></script> <script type= "text/javascript" src= "dist/TableCheckAll.js" ></script> <script type= "text/javascript" > $ ( document ). ready ( function () { var tableSelector = ' #permissions-table ' ; $ . each ( $ ( ' .select-all-module-permissions ' ). parents ( ' tr ' ), function () { var that = $ ( this ); var selectAllPermissionsClass = that . find ( ' .select-all-module-permissions ' ). attr ( ' data-class ' ); $ . each ( that . find ( ' .select-module-permission ' ), function () { var selectPermissionClass = $ ( this ). attr ( ' data-class ' ); $ ( tableSelector ). TableCheckAll ({ checkAllCheckboxClass : ' . ' + selectAllPermissionsClass , checkboxClass : ' . ' + selectPermissionClass }); }); }); $ ( tableSelector ). TableCheckAll ({ checkAllCheckboxClass : ' .select-all-permissions ' , checkboxClass : ' .select-permission ' }); }); </script> </head> <body> <div class= "container mt-5" > <form> <div class= "form-group" > <label> Role </label> <input type= "text" class= "form-control" > </div><br><br> <h3> Assign Permissions </h3> <table class= "table table-striped" id= "permissions-table" > <thead> <tr> <th scope= "col" > Module </th> <th scope= "col" ><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-all-permissions" > Select All </th> <th scope= "col" > Available Permissions </th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <th scope= "row" > Clients </th> <td><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-all-module-permissions select-all-client-permissions" data-class= "select-all-client-permissions" > Select All </td> <td><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-module-permission select-client-permission" data-class= "select-client-permission" > Create Client <input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-module-permission select-client-permission" data-class= "select-client-permission" > Edit Client </td> </tr> <tr> <th scope= "row" > Blogs </th> <td><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-all-module-permissions select-all-blogs-permissions" data-class= "select-all-blogs-permissions" > Select All </td> <td><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-module-permission select-blogs-permission" data-class= "select-blogs-permission" > Create Blog <input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-module-permission select-blogs-permission" data-class= "select-blogs-permission" > Edit Blog </td> </tr> <tr> <th scope= "row" > Users </th> <td><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-all-module-permissions select-all-users-permissions" data-class= "select-all-users-permissions" > Select All </td> <td><input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-module-permission select-users-permission" data-class= "select-users-permission" > Create User <input type= "checkbox" class= "select-permission select-module-permission select-users-permission" data-class= "select-users-permission" > Edit User </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> </form> <br> <br> <br> </div> </body> </html>

我希望本教程可以帮助你。 如果您想下载此代码，请访问此处https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/jquery/jquery-checkbox-assign-multiple-permissions-to-a-role 。

快乐编码:)



原文:

Originally posted @ https://codeanddeploy.com visit and download the sample code:
https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/jquery/jquery-checkbox-assign-multiple-permissions-to-a-role

In this post, I will share a simple code on how to implement a jquery checkbox to assign multiple permissions to a role. In case you are implementing multiple permissions with a checkbox to a role then this post might help you.

Previously, I posted about how to implement the jQuery TableCheckAll plugin which I created. You can visit here the previous post.

Okay, you have the idea already what is the expected output. Let's process how to do it.

First, Clone the TableCheckAll jquery Plugin. 

git clone https://github.com/codeanddeploy/Jquery-Table-Check-All-Plugin.git

Once you downloaded it, create a new file inside the folder you downloaded. Let's say index.html then paste the code below:

Second, Here is the full source code

In this section, we have a sample source code that can implement to your project.

Take note: The .select-all-permissions and .select-permission classes are able to select all modules permissions. So you don't need to replace these classes.

For .select-permission class, you need to add this in every module permission checkbox including the module select all permission checkbox. As you can see in this code:

<tr> <th scope="row">Clients</th> <td><input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-all-module-permissions select-all-client-permissions" data-class="select-all-client-permissions"> Select All</td> <td><input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-module-permission select-client-permission" data-class="select-client-permission"> Create Client <input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-module-permission select-client-permission" data-class="select-client-permission"> Edit Client</td>
</tr>

To trigger the module permission we need also to add class .select-module-permission and specific class for each module permission with custom attribute data-class="select-{module_name}-permission" and class name .select-{module_name}-permission.

Take note that the {module_name} is your module name without space.

Then in each module select all checkbox. You need to add .select-all-module-permissions class name. Because we use this to loop all available modules.

Then next we will add a custom attribute data-class="select-all-{module_name}-permissions" and add a class .select-all-{module_name}-permissions in each module select all checkbox.

Here is the full source code.

<!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <meta charset="utf-8" /> <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"> <title>Jquery Checkbox Assign Multiple Permissions to a Role - codeanddeploy.com</title> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"> <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.0.2/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css"> <script src="https://ajax.googleapis.com/ajax/libs/jquery/3.5.1/jquery.min.js"></script> <script type="text/javascript" src="dist/TableCheckAll.js"></script> <script type="text/javascript"> $(document).ready(function() { var tableSelector = '#permissions-table'; $.each( $( '.select-all-module-permissions' ).parents('tr'), function() { var that = $(this); var selectAllPermissionsClass = that.find('.select-all-module-permissions').attr('data-class'); $.each(that.find('.select-module-permission'), function() { var selectPermissionClass = $(this).attr('data-class'); $( tableSelector ).TableCheckAll({ checkAllCheckboxClass: '.' + selectAllPermissionsClass, checkboxClass: '.' + selectPermissionClass }); }); }); $( tableSelector ).TableCheckAll({ checkAllCheckboxClass: '.select-all-permissions', checkboxClass: '.select-permission' }); }); </script> </head> <body> <div class="container mt-5"> <form> <div class="form-group"> <label>Role</label> <input type="text" class="form-control"> </div><br><br> <h3>Assign Permissions</h3> <table class="table table-striped" id="permissions-table"> <thead> <tr> <th scope="col">Module</th> <th scope="col"><input type="checkbox" class="select-all-permissions"> Select All</th> <th scope="col">Available Permissions</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <th scope="row">Clients</th> <td><input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-all-module-permissions select-all-client-permissions" data-class="select-all-client-permissions"> Select All</td> <td><input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-module-permission select-client-permission" data-class="select-client-permission"> Create Client <input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-module-permission select-client-permission" data-class="select-client-permission"> Edit Client</td> </tr> <tr> <th scope="row">Blogs</th> <td><input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-all-module-permissions select-all-blogs-permissions" data-class="select-all-blogs-permissions"> Select All</td> <td><input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-module-permission select-blogs-permission" data-class="select-blogs-permission"> Create Blog <input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-module-permission select-blogs-permission" data-class="select-blogs-permission"> Edit Blog</td> </tr> <tr> <th scope="row">Users</th> <td><input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-all-module-permissions select-all-users-permissions" data-class="select-all-users-permissions"> Select All</td> <td><input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-module-permission select-users-permission" data-class="select-users-permission"> Create User <input type="checkbox" class="select-permission select-module-permission select-users-permission" data-class="select-users-permission"> Edit User</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> </form> <br> <br> <br> </div> </body>
</html>

I hope this tutorial can help you. Kindly visit here https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/jquery/jquery-checkbox-assign-multiple-permissions-to-a-role if you want to download this code.

Happy coding :)
