item.title: 应用服务字典。 | App Service Dictionary.
item.description:
在这篇文章中，将解释从使用应用服务中学到的一些术语，初学者在导航或创建应用服务时会发现这些术语很有用。

平台即服务

平台即服务是一种 Azure 产品，可帮助您在 Web 上部署应用程序，而无需担心许可、Windows 或 Linux 版本或更新您的操作系统。 它可以帮助您管理底层基础架构并让您专注于部署应用程序。

这是一个来自 azure 的平台即服务 (PAAS)，它允许您使用 GitHub 或其他代码托管平台在 azure 上部署您的代码，它带有许多预构建的功能，可以轻松部署您的应用程序，例如身份验证、API管理、CI/CD 等

资源组

资源组是一个逻辑容器，用于在 Azure 上存储存储、应用服务、虚拟网络等资源。
资源组允许您管理所有资源、管理权限和监控成本。

区域冗余

为了使您的应用程序在部署应用程序的特定位置出现停机时可用，您使用区域冗余，这是在创建应用程序时完成的，它用于在 3 个不同的天蓝色区域部署应用程序的副本，以便在一个区域发生故障的情况下，另一个应用程序实例继续为应用程序提供服务。

部署槽

如果您正在尝试测试您之前构建的更改，或者您希望某些更改可供访问您的 Web 应用程序的某些人使用，那么部署槽就是您所需要的。 它可以帮助您预览对您的应用所做的更改，并与您的一些网站访问者分享这些更改，以获得对您的应用的反馈。
您可以通过增加发送到创建的新插槽的流量来做到这一点，默认数字是 0%。



原文:

In this post would be explaining some term learned from working with app service that beginner would find useful when navigating or creating an app Service.

Platform as a Service

Platform as a Service is an Azure offering that helps you deploy application on the web without worrying about licensing, Windows or Linux version or Updating your Operating System. It helps you manage the underlying infrastructure and allows you to focus on deploying application.

Azure App Service

This is a Platform as a Service (PAAS) from azure that allows you to deploy your code on azure using GitHub or other Code hosting platform, it comes with a lot of pre built functions that makes deploying your app easy, examples are Authentication, API management, CI/CD etc.

Resource Group

Resource group is a logical container used to store resources like Storage, App Service, Virtual Network etc. on Azure.
Resource group allows you to manage all resources, manage Permission and Monitor Cost.

Zone Redundancy

To make your app available if there is a downtime in a particular location where your app has been deployed, you use Zone Redundancy, this is done when creating your app and it is used to deploy a copy of your app in 3 different azure zone, so that in case of a failure in a a zone another app instance continue to serve the application.

Deployment Slot

If you are trying to test changes you made you previously built or you want some changes to be available to some people who visit your web app, then deployment slot is what you need. It helps you preview changes made to your app, and also share this changes with some of your website visitors to get feedback on your app.
You do this by increase the traffic sent to the new slot created, the default number is 0 percent.
item.author: osuolalee

item.title: 前端新闻#064 | Google I/O 2022 Roundup, the State of Frontend in 2022, Edge Secure Network, Polypane 9, and more | Front End News #064
item.description:
注意：这是我的时事通讯的第 064 期，于 5 月 16 日星期一上线。 如果您发现此信息有用且有趣，并且希望在发布后收到未来的问题，那么我邀请您加入frontendnexus.com上的订阅者列表。

软件更新部分包含 Hexo 6.2、pnpm v7.1、Solid v1.4 等。

谷歌 I/O 2022 综述

幸运的是，web.dev 团队为我们提供了一些关于网络相关主题、公告和主题演讲的专门文章，以下是重点：

  • 网络性能仍然是一个关键的兴趣
  • 将继续开发新的和现有的 Web API，以扩展网站和 Web 应用程序与操作系统和硬件集成的方式
  • 隐私沙箱将得到更多关注，这是“在没有第三方 cookie 或其他跟踪机制的情况下满足跨站点用例的一系列建议”。
  • 去年所有浏览器的 CSS 支持都有很大改进
  • 甚至还有专门的“CSS 2022 状态”文章探讨了新的和即将推出的样式 API
  • 有很多信息需要查看，在大多数情况下，我们只获得了活动中演示的视频记录。 我在下面链接了尽可能多的资源。 我期待很快会有更多。

    软件之家发布了一份关于 2022 年前端状态的综合研究报告，该报告收集了来自 125 个国家/地区的 3700 多人的数据，并得到了一些业内大腕的分析和评论。 您可能认识像 Chris Coyier、Sébastien Chopin（Nuxt 的作者）或 Olivier Tassinari（Material-UI 的共同创建者）这样的人。 仅凭他们的洞察力，这份报告就值得一读。

    浏览器新闻

    边缘

    该功能称为“边缘安全网络”，由 Cloudflare 提供支持，每月包含 1GB 的数据流量。 该功能仍在开发中，需要使用 Microsoft 帐户。

    聚苯板

    屏幕截图生成速度更快，您可以在新的屏幕截图编辑器中进行编辑

  • 适合移动设备的测试仪可帮助您发现按钮太小或彼此太靠近等问题
  • 在大纲面板中获得更好、更有用的警告
  • 支持color-scheme元属性

您可以在下面链接的博客文章中找到有关这些功能以及更新中包含的所有其他内容的更多详细信息。

  • 软件更新和发布

    收拾东西

    乌克兰仍在遭受俄罗斯入侵 - 如果您正在寻找帮助的方法，请查看 Smashing Magazine 的文章We All Are Ukraine 🇺🇦或与您信任的慈善机构联系。

    如果您喜欢本时事通讯，有几种方法可以支持它。 您可以在社交媒体上分享此问题的链接，并在Twitter 上关注此时事通讯。 这些都对我有帮助，我会很感激你的考虑。

    这就是我对这个问题的全部了解。 祝您度过愉快而富有成效的一周，确保自己的安全，尽可能多地与您所爱的人共度时光，我们下次再见！

帕特里克·瓦韦鲁 - Jun 25 '21 21 年 6 月 25 日

Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - Jun 24 '21 21 年 6 月 24 日

Jyotishman Saikia - Jyotishman Saikia - Jun 5 '21 21 年 6 月 5 日

Safari Technology Preview 144, Mocha 10, npm 8.9.0, and more | Front End News #063 64 Google I/O 2022 Roundup, the State of Frontend in 2022, Edge Secure Network, Polypane 9, and more | Front End News #064

NOTE: This is issue #064 of my newsletter, which went live on Monday, May 16th. If you find this information useful and interesting and you want to receive future issues as they are published, ahead of everyone else, I invite you to join the subscriber list at frontendnexus.com.

Google I/O 2022 is now over and we get a round-up of all topics related to the web industry, including a report on the State of CSS in 2022. The Software House published a comprehensive study on the State of Frontend in 2022. Microsoft is adding a free built-in VPN to the Edge browser. Polypane 9 has been released. And the software updates section features Hexo 6.2, pnpm v7.1, Solid v1.4, and more.

Google I/O 2022 Roundup

Every year Google runs its I/O developer event and the 2022 edition just ended. The Google ecosystem is vast, and it covers many areas. Fortunately, the web.dev team provided us with some dedicated articles just on the web-related topics, announcements, and keynotes, and here are the highlights:

  • web performance remains a key interest
  • the Core Web Vitals receive a new metric named Interaction to Next Paint (INP)
  • work will continue on new and existing Web APIs to expand the way websites and web apps can integrate with the operating system and the hardware
  • there will be more focus on the Privacy Sandbox, which is "a series of proposals to satisfy cross-site use cases without third-party cookies or other tracking mechanisms."
  • there have been large improvements in CSS support over the last year across all browsers
  • there is even a dedicated "State of CSS 2022" article that explores the new and upcoming styling APIs
  • The Chrome team works with other browser vendors to make it easier to develop for the web

There is a lot of information to go through and, in most cases, we only got the video recordings of the presentations made at the event. I've linked below as many resources as I could find. I am expecting more to come soon.

State of frontend 2022

The Software House published a comprehensive study on the State of Frontend in 2022. The report collected data from over 3700 people in 125 countries, and it has been analyzed and commented on by some big names in the industry. You might recognize people like Chris Coyier, Sébastien Chopin (author of Nuxt), or Olivier Tassinari (co-creator of Material-UI). Their insight alone makes this report worth reading.

Browser news

Edge

The Verge is reporting that Microsoft is adding a free built-in VPN to the Edge browser. The feature is called "Edge Secure Network", it is powered by Cloudflare, and it includes 1GB of data traffic each month. The functionality is still under development, and it requires the use of a Microsoft Account.

Polypane

Polypane, the "browser for ambitious developers", has just received the update to version 9. Here are a few highlights of the new features available:

  • screenshots are generated faster, and you can edit them in the new screenshot editor
  • the mobile-friendly tester helps you find issues such as buttons being too small or too close one to another
  • get better and more useful warnings in the outline panel
  • support for the color-scheme meta attribute

You can find more details on these features and everything else included in the update in the blog post linked below.

  • Polypane 9: Screenshot editor, structured data support, and new debug tools

Software updates and releases

Wrapping things up

Ukraine is still suffering from the Russian invasion - if you are looking for ways to help, please check Smashing Magazine's article We All Are Ukraine 🇺🇦 or get in touch with your trusted charity.

If you enjoyed this newsletter, there are a couple of ways to support it. You can share the link to this issue on social media and follow this newsletter on Twitter. Each of these helps me out, and I would appreciate your consideration.

That's about all I have for this issue. Have a great and productive week, keep yourselves safe, spend as much time as possible with your loved ones, and I will see you again next time!

item.title: Laravel 8 集合与 first() 和 firstWhere() 方法示例 | Laravel 8 Collection with first() and firstWhere() Methods Example
item.description:
代码和部署

发表于原贴@https ://codeanddeploy.com访问并下载示例代码：
在这个例子中，我将向你展示如何使用first()和irstWhere()方法来实现Laravel 8 Collection 。 在做一个 Laravel 项目时，我们应该使用 Laravel 集合来更容易地处理我们的数组数据。 我们将使用 Laravel 的collect辅助函数从我们的数组中创建一个新的集合实例。

有时您需要获取第一个元素或使用条件查询来显示我们 Laravel 集合的第一个结果。 使用first()和firstWhere()方法将使我们更容易。

示例 1：Laravel 集合 first() 方法示例

public function index () { $collection = collect ([ [ 'id' => 1 , 'name' => 'Juan Dela Cruz' , 'age' => 25 ], [ 'id' => 2 , 'name' => 'Juana Vasquez' , 'age' => 31 ], [ 'id' => 3 , 'name' => 'Jason Reyes' , 'age' => 27 ], [ 'id' => 4 , 'name' => 'Jake Ramos' , 'age' => 43 ], ]); $first = $collection -> first (); dd ( $first ); }

输出：

 array : 3 [ ▼ "id" => 1 "name" => "Juan Dela Cruz" "age" => 25 ]

示例 2：Laravel 集合 first() 方法示例

public function index () { $collection = collect ([ [ 'id' => 1 , 'name' => 'Juan Dela Cruz' , 'age' => 25 , 'gender' => 'Male' ], [ 'id' => 2 , 'name' => 'Juana Vasquez' , 'age' => 31 , 'gender' => 'Female' ], [ 'id' => 3 , 'name' => 'Jason Reyes' , 'age' => 27 , 'gender' => 'Male' ], [ 'id' => 4 , 'name' => 'Jake Ramos' , 'age' => 43 , 'gender' => 'Male' ], ]); $first = $collection -> firstWhere ( 'name' , 'Juan Dela Cruz' ); dd ( $first ); }

输出：

就是这样，我希望你学习如何使用 first() 和 firstWhere() 方法获取 Laravel 集合的第一个元素。

我希望本教程可以帮助你。 如果您想下载此代码，请访问此处https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/laravel-8-collection-with-first-and-firstwhere-methods-example 。

快乐编码:)

鲁贝姆·瓦斯康塞洛斯 - May 9 5月9日



原文:

codeanddeploy

Posted on May 16

Originally posted @ https://codeanddeploy.com visit and download the sample code:
https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/laravel-8-collection-with-first-and-firstwhere-methods-example

In this example, I will show you how to implement the Laravel 8 Collection with first() and firstWhere() methods. When doing a Laravel project we should use Laravel collection for easier to process our array data. We will use the collect helper function of Laravel to create a new collection instance from our array.

And sometimes you need to get the first element or with a condition query that will display the first result of our Laravel collection. With the use of first() and firstWhere() methods will make it easier for us.

Example 1: Laravel Collection first() Method Example 

public function index()
{ $collection = collect([ ['id'=> 1, 'name'=>'Juan Dela Cruz', 'age' => 25], ['id'=> 2, 'name'=>'Juana Vasquez', 'age' => 31], ['id'=> 3, 'name'=>'Jason Reyes', 'age' => 27], ['id'=> 4, 'name'=>'Jake Ramos', 'age' => 43], ]); $first = $collection->first(); dd($first);
}

Output:

array:3 [▼ "id" => 1 "name" => "Juan Dela Cruz" "age" => 25
]

Example 2: Laravel Collection first() Method Example 

public function index()
{ $collection = collect([ ['id'=> 1, 'name'=>'Juan Dela Cruz', 'age' => 25, 'gender' => 'Male'], ['id'=> 2, 'name'=>'Juana Vasquez', 'age' => 31, 'gender' => 'Female'], ['id'=> 3, 'name'=>'Jason Reyes', 'age' => 27, 'gender' => 'Male'], ['id'=> 4, 'name'=>'Jake Ramos', 'age' => 43, 'gender' => 'Male'], ]); $first = $collection->firstWhere('name', 'Juan Dela Cruz'); dd($first);
}

Output:

That's it I hope you learn how to get the first element of the Laravel collection using first() and firstWhere() methods.

I hope this tutorial can help you. Kindly visit here https://codeanddeploy.com/blog/laravel/laravel-8-collection-with-first-and-firstwhere-methods-example if you want to download this code.

Happy coding :)

Rubem Vasconcelos - May 9

item.title: Frontmatter 到底是什么？ | What exactly is Frontmatter?
item.description:
当我写包含 Markdown 文件或数据的文章时，我经常提到Frontmatter 。

什么是前端？

但是这个 Frontmatter 到底是什么？

元数据实际上可以是您想要的任何东西，但通常它用于您的页面需要并且您不想直接显示的数据元素。

常见元数据的一些示例包括：

  • 帖子标题
  • 用于 SEO 目的的说明
  • 属于文档的标签
  • 或者写的日期

这些只是一些示例，您可以使用 Frontmatter 让它变得疯狂。

如何添加 Frontmatter？

要将 Frontmatter 添加到 Markdown 文档中，您必须首先在文件顶部编写一个三点块。

 --- title : " Frontmatter section " --- # Markdown section

您可以在上面的示例中看到三个点作为示例。 那里的所有内容都将被视为元数据。

在第二个三个点下方是我们的默认 Markdown 文件将开始的位置。

一些基本的 Frontmatter 选项

需要注意的是，Frontmatter 被解析为 YAML 块，因此缩进很重要。

例如，我们可以使用冒号设置常规变量：

 --- title : ' The document title ' ---

或者我们可以将它们转换为对象数组：

 --- tags : - JavaScript - Markdown ---

或者，您甚至可以使用定义数组的括号方式。

 --- tags : [ " JavaScript " , " Markdown " ] ---

在极端情况下，您甚至可能想要拥有多维对象数组。

 --- tech : - frontend stack : Remix - backend stack : Go ---

除了数组，您可能还想使用多行文本块。

添加它的第一种方法只是在您的代码编辑器中可视化。 这不会在输出的代码中添加换行符。

 --- description : > this is a multiline string ---

它将输出： this is a multi-line string

但是，如果要添加新行，请使用管道方法。

 --- description : | this is a multiline string ---

这将输出：

 this is a multi-line string

结论

它可以增强您的 SEO、网站数据，甚至呈现正确的 MDX 属性。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

When I'm writing my articles that include Markdown files or data, I often refer to Frontmatter.

What is Frontmatter?

But what is this Frontmatter exactly?

Frontmatter is a way to identify metadata in Markdown files.
Metadata can literally be anything you want it to be, but often it's used for data elements your page needs and you don't want to show directly.

Some examples of common metadata are:

  • Title of the post
  • Description for SEO purposes
  • Tags that belong to a document
  • Or the date it was written

These are only some examples, and you can make it as crazy as you want with Frontmatter.

How to add Frontmatter?

To add Frontmatter to a Markdown document, you have to start by writing a three-dotted block at the top of your file.

---
title: "Frontmatter section"
--- # Markdown section

You can see the three dots as an example in the above example. Everything in there will be seen as metadata.

Below the second three dots is where our default Markdown file will begin.

Some basic Frontmatter options

It's important to note that Frontmatter is parsed as YAML blocks, so the indentation is important.

For instance, we can set regular variables with a colon setup:

---
title: 'The document title'
---

Or we can convert them into an array of objects:

---
tags: - JavaScript - Markdown
---

Alternatively, you can even use the bracket way of defining arrays.

---
tags: ["JavaScript", "Markdown"]
---

In extreme cases, you might even want to have multidimensional object arrays.

---
tech: - frontend stack: Remix - backend stack: Go
---

Besides arrays, you might want to use multi-line text blocks.

The first way to add this is only visually in your code editor. This will not add newlines in the outputted code.

---
description: > this is a multiline string
---

Which will output: this is a multi-line string

However, if you want to add new lines, use the pipe method.

---
description: | this is a multiline string
---

Which will output:

this is a
multi-line
string

Conclusion

Frontmatter is a super intuitive way of making your Markdown files contain some metadata.

It can enhance your SEO, Website data and even render proper MDX properties.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter
item.author: dailydevtips1

item.title: RADU：使用 Python 使用游戏手柄控制器控制机器人运动 | RADU: Controlling Robot Movements with a Gamepad Controller using Python
item.description:
我的机器人 RADU 是一个两轮机器人，它结合了传感器和电机的定制硬件、Raspberry Pico 和 L293D 电机护罩，以及通过 Raspberry Pi 护罩控制的可购买机械臂。 对于机器人的两个部分，我想使用相同的控制机制和设备：游戏控制器。 为了达到这个目标，上一篇文章概述了不同的库。 本文是关于编写控制器软件的教程。 您将学习如何使用 Python 库Piborg Gamepad ，以及如何桥接按下的按钮或操纵杆来控制机器人的轮子运动及其手臂。

这篇文章最初出现在我的博客admantium.com上。

游戏手柄库

我选择的 Gamepad 库是Piborg Gamepad 。 这个多功能的 Python 库提供了简单的抽象来连接自定义映射游戏手柄，稍微关注视频游戏机。 它可以检测所有按钮和轴的状态，例如在 PlayStation pad 上，包括 d-pad 和模拟摇杆。 它提供了不同的编程模型如何访问游戏手柄状态，例如使用触发回调的事件侦听器，或获取所有按钮状态的连续循环。

经过很短的时间后，只需按下一个简单的按钮即可移动机器人，我决定使用这个库来对我的机器人进行完全编程。 让我们从简单的轮廓开始。

按钮配置

第一部分是为所选游戏手柄的按钮导入库和设置变量。 为此，请检查游戏手柄配置并从此文件中为您的特定游戏手柄获取定义的按钮名称。 然后，将这些翻译成有意义的名称。 这是我访问操纵杆、数字键盘和一些按钮的映射：

 import lib.Gamepad as Gamepad # Gamepad settings gamepadType = Gamepad . PS4 # Axis joystickSpeed = 'LEFT-Y' joystickSteering = 'RIGHT-X' digital_x = 'DPAD-X' digital_y = 'DPAD-Y' # Button button_ps = 'PS' button_triangle = "TRIANGLE"

控制器连接

完成按钮设置后，我们现在将等待编写一个等待游戏手柄连接的方法。 一旦这个条件成立，我们将创建一个使用配置实例化的游戏手柄对象。 此代码如下所示：

 def wait_for_gamepad (): # Wait for a connection if not Gamepad . available (): log_to_stdout_and_mqtt ( 'Please connect your gamepad...' ) while not Gamepad . available (): time . sleep ( 1.0 ) gamepad = gamepadType () return gamepad

检测游戏手柄按钮按下

下一步是启动程序的主循环，该循环将不断检查按钮状态。 我决定使用一个简单的循环来不断地轮询按钮并执行一些命令。 让我们开始简单并为按钮定义一个侦听器。

 def main_loop ( gamepad ) pollInterval = 0.01 gamepad . startBackgroundUpdates () try : while gamepad . isConnected (): if gamepad . beenPressed ( button_triangle ): log_to_stdout ( "Triangle Button is Pressed" ) # Sleep for our polling interval time . sleep ( pollInterval ) finally : # Ensure the background thread is always terminated when we are done gamepad . disconnect ()

这个方法做了几件事：

  • 完整的按钮检测逻辑包含在try...catch块中，主要是为了在出现问题时释放游戏手柄 - 或者您可以定义自定义上下文管理器来处理设置和拆卸阶段
  • 使用beenPressed()方法检查游戏手柄按钮状态，该方法获取先前定义的按钮布局的参数。 如此处所示，您可以使用if语句来检查它们的状态，然后执行一些命令
  • 在循环结束时，使用定义的pollInterval发出sleep命令

好的，现在让我们看看如何检查 d-pad 和转向轴。

检测数字和模拟轴

在按钮设置期间，我们已经为模拟操纵杆和方向键定义了自定义名称。 然后，在主循环中，我们可以检测它们的状态，如下一个代码片段所示。

 def main_loop ( gamepad ) #... try : while gamepad . isConnected (): # ... speed = - gamepad . axis ( joystickSpeed ) steering = - gamepad . axis ( joystickSteering ) command = gamepad . axis ( digital_x ) log_to_stdout ( f 'Speed { speed } ' ) log_to_stdout ( f 'Steering { steering } ' ) engine_move ( speed , steering , command ) time . sleep ( pollInterval ) finally : # Ensure the background thread is always terminated when we are done gamepad . disconnect ()

要访问游戏手柄值，请使用axis()方法并传递按钮名称。 这些值是不同粒度的浮点数：对于 d-pad 按钮，它是0.0或1.0 ，但对于模拟摇杆，它是一个正数或负数 16 位数字，最大绝对值为32.756 。 处理机器人需要的这些值。

微调控件

此步骤在很大程度上取决于您的特定机器人的行为。 我的机器人不断地侦听命令，处理它们，并在发送者站点不活动 300 毫秒后退出命令。 这意味着发送者需要连续发送操纵杆的位置，以便让机器人仅在实际按下任何一个时才移动。

此要求由以下代码部分提供：

 def main_loop ( gamepad ) #... try : while gamepad . isConnected (): if ( mode == MOVE_MODE ): speed = - gamepad . axis ( joystickSpeed ) steering = - gamepad . axis ( joystickSteering ) if (( speed != 0.0 or steering != 0.0 ) and not isStopped ): last_speed = speed last_steeering = steering engine_move ( speed , steering ) else : isStopped = True time . sleep ( pollInterval ) finally : # Ensure the background thread is always terminated when we are done gamepad . disconnect ()

控制手臂运动

一旦我们弄清楚如何读取和微调控制器值以移动机器人，让我们创建附加功能来控制机器人手臂。 我不会详细介绍实际移动手臂的手臂微控制器代码，而只关注游戏手柄代码。

我们需要的第一个功能是区分不同的控制器模式。 此状态保存在以下变量中：

 MOVE_MODE = 'MOVE_MODE' ARM_MODE = 'ARM_MODE'

在主循环内部，我们需要做如下扩展来改变当前的控制模式：

 def main_loop ( gamepad ) #... while gamepad . isConnected (): if gamepad . beenPressed ( button_ps ): if ( mode == MOVE_MODE ): mode = ARM_MODE else : mode = MOVE_MODE

最后，主循环区分控制模式，当手臂模式处于活动状态时，它将解析用于移动手臂的操纵杆的值和用于移动夹具的数字键盘的值：

 def main_loop ( gamepad ) #... try : while gamepad . isConnected (): #... if ( mode == MOVE_MODE ): # ... if ( mode == ARM_MODE ): gripper_x = gamepad . axis ( digital_x ) gripper_y = - gamepad . axis ( digital_y ) if ( gripper_x != 0.0 ): arm . move_incr ( SERVO_G , 4 * int ( gripper_x )) log_to_stdout_and_mqtt ( "X {}, Y {}" . format ( gripper_x , gripper_y )) if ( gripper_y != - 0.0 ): arm . move_incr ( SERVO_Z , 4 * int ( gripper_y )) log_to_stdout_and_mqtt ( "X {}, Y {}" . format ( gripper_x , gripper_y )) arm_x = gamepad . axis ( joystickSteering ) arm_y = - gamepad . axis ( joystickSpeed ) if ( arm_x != 0.0 ): arm . move_incr ( SERVO_X , int ( 100 * arm_x ) / 4 ) log_to_stdout_and_mqtt ( "SERVO_X {} => {}" . format ( arm_x , int ( 100 * arm_x ) / 4 )) if ( arm_y != - 0.0 ): arm . move_incr ( SERVO_Y , int ( 100 * arm_y ) / 4 ) log_to_stdout_and_mqtt ( "SERVO_Y {} => {}" . format ( arm_y , int ( 100 * arm_y ) / 4 )) if gamepad . beenPressed ( button_triangle ): arm . center () time . sleep ( pollInterval ) finally : # Ensure the background thread is always terminated when we are done gamepad . disconnect ()

结论

本文展示了如何使用 Python 库Piborg Gamepad 。 您学习了如何定义按钮布局，如何通过等待连接的游戏手柄启动程序，以及如何持续检查按钮、数字键盘和操纵杆的状态。 将所有这些放在一起是一个小而易读的脚本，您可以根据要求或您的机器人进行自定义。 这篇文章具体展示了如何使用这个库来控制我的机器人 RADU 的轮子运动和手臂运动。 在下一篇也是最后一篇文章中，我将总结我开发定制机器人的历程。



原文:

My robot RADU is a two wheeled robot that combines custom hardware for the sensor and motor, a Raspberry Pico and L293D motor shield, and a purchasable robotic arm that is controlled via a Raspberry Pi shield. For both parts of the robot, I want to use the same control mechanism and device: A game controller. To reach this goal, the last article gave an overview about the different libraries. This article is a tutorial about writing the controller software. You will learn how to use the Python library Piborg Gamepad, and how to bridge pressed buttons or joystick to control the robots wheel movements and its arm.

This article originally appeared at my blog admantium.com.

The Gamepad Library

The Gamepad Library of my choice is the Piborg Gamepad. This versatile Python library provides simple abstraction to connect custom mapped gamepads, with a slight focus on video game consoles. It can detect the state of all buttons and the axis, e.g. on a PlayStation pad including the d-pad and the analog stick. And it provides different programming models how to access the gamepad state, e.g. with event listeners that trigger callbacks, or a continuous loop that gets the state of all buttons.

After a short time period only to get a simple button press into moving the robot, I decided to use this library to fully program my robot. Let’s start with the simple outline.

Button Configuration

The first part is to import the library and setup variables for the buttons of the chosen gamepad. For this, checkout the gamepad configurations and take the defined button names from this file for your specific gamepad. Then, translate these to meaningful names. Here is my mapping for accessing the joystick, the digital pad and some buttons:

import lib.Gamepad as Gamepad # Gamepad settings
gamepadType = Gamepad.PS4 # Axis
joystickSpeed = 'LEFT-Y'
joystickSteering = 'RIGHT-X' digital_x = 'DPAD-X' digital_y = 'DPAD-Y' # Button
button_ps = 'PS'
button_triangle = "TRIANGLE"

Controller Connection

With the button setup completed, we will now wait write a method that waits for a gamepad to get connected. Once this condition is true, we will create a gamepad object that is instantiated with the configuration. This code looks as follows:

def wait_for_gamepad(): # Wait for a connection
 if not Gamepad.available(): log_to_stdout_and_mqtt('Please connect your gamepad...') while not Gamepad.available(): time.sleep(1.0) gamepad = gamepadType() return gamepad

Detecting Gamepad Button Presses

The next step is to start the main loop of the program that will continually check the button state. I decided to use a simple loop that continually polls the buttons and executes some commands. Let’s start easy and define a listener for the buttons.

def main_loop(gamepad) pollInterval = 0.01 gamepad.startBackgroundUpdates() try: while gamepad.isConnected(): if gamepad.beenPressed(button_triangle): log_to_stdout("Triangle Button is Pressed") # Sleep for our polling interval
 time.sleep(pollInterval) finally: # Ensure the background thread is always terminated when we are done
 gamepad.disconnect()

This method does a couple of things:

  • The pollInterval determines how often per second the button states will be checked
  • The complete button detection logic is enclosed in a try...catch block, mainly as a safeguard to release the gamepad if something goes wrong - alternatively you can define a custom context manager to handle the setup and teardown phases
  • A gamepads button state are checked with the beenPressed() method, which gets an argument of the earlier defined button layout. As shown here, you can use if statements to check their state, and then execute some command
  • At the end of the loop, a sleep command is issued with the defined pollInterval

Ok, now let’s see how to check for the d-pad and steering axes.

Detecting the Digital and Analog Axis

During the button setup, we already defined custom names for the analog joysticks and the d-pad. Then, inside the main loop, we can detect their state as shown in the next code snippet.

def main_loop(gamepad) #...
 try: while gamepad.isConnected(): # ...
 speed = - gamepad.axis(joystickSpeed) steering = - gamepad.axis(joystickSteering) command = gamepad.axis(digital_x) log_to_stdout(f'Speed {speed}') log_to_stdout(f'Steering {steering}') engine_move(speed, steering, command) time.sleep(pollInterval) finally: # Ensure the background thread is always terminated when we are done
 gamepad.disconnect()

To access the gamepad values, you use the axis() method and pass the button name. The values are floats of different granularly: For the d-pad buttons, its either 0.0 or 1.0, but for the analog sticks, it’s a positive or negative 16-bit number with a maximum absolute value of 32.756. Handle these values as they are required by your robot.

Fine Tuning the Controls

This step is heavily dependent on your particular robots’ behavior. My robot continually listens for commands, processes them, and quits the command after 300ms of inactivity from the sender site. This means that the sender needs to continuously send the position of the joystick in order to let the robot only move when either is actually pressed.

This requirement is provided by the following code section:

def main_loop(gamepad) #...
 try: while gamepad.isConnected(): if (mode == MOVE_MODE): speed = -gamepad.axis(joystickSpeed) steering = -gamepad.axis(joystickSteering) if((speed != 0.0 or steering != 0.0) and not isStopped): last_speed = speed last_steeering = steering engine_move(speed, steering) else: isStopped = True time.sleep(pollInterval) finally: # Ensure the background thread is always terminated when we are done
 gamepad.disconnect()

Controlling the Arm Movements

Once we figured out how to read and fine-tune the controller values to move the robots, lets create the additional feature to controls the robots arm as well. I will not detail the arm's microcontroller code that actually moves the arm, but focus on the gamepad code only.

The first feature that we need is to distinguish between different controller modes. This state is kept in the following variables:

MOVE_MODE = 'MOVE_MODE'
ARM_MODE = 'ARM_MODE'

Inside the main loop, we need to make the following extension to change the current control mode:

def main_loop(gamepad) #...
 while gamepad.isConnected(): if gamepad.beenPressed(button_ps): if (mode == MOVE_MODE): mode = ARM_MODE else: mode = MOVE_MODE

Finally, the main loop distinguishes between the controls mode, and when the arm mode is active, it will parse the value of the joysticks for moving the arm, and of the digital pad for moving the gripper:

def main_loop(gamepad) #...
 try: while gamepad.isConnected(): #...
 if (mode == MOVE_MODE): # ...
 if (mode == ARM_MODE): gripper_x = gamepad.axis(digital_x) gripper_y = -gamepad.axis(digital_y) if (gripper_x != 0.0): arm.move_incr(SERVO_G, 4*int(gripper_x)) log_to_stdout_and_mqtt("X {}, Y {}".format(gripper_x, gripper_y)) if (gripper_y != -0.0): arm.move_incr(SERVO_Z, 4*int(gripper_y )) log_to_stdout_and_mqtt("X {}, Y {}".format(gripper_x, gripper_y )) arm_x = gamepad.axis(joystickSteering) arm_y = -gamepad.axis(joystickSpeed) if (arm_x != 0.0): arm.move_incr(SERVO_X, int(100*arm_x)/4) log_to_stdout_and_mqtt("SERVO_X {} => {}".format(arm_x, int(100*arm_x)/4)) if (arm_y != -0.0): arm.move_incr(SERVO_Y, int(100*arm_y)/4) log_to_stdout_and_mqtt("SERVO_Y {} => {}".format(arm_y,int(100*arm_y)/4)) if gamepad.beenPressed(button_triangle): arm.center() time.sleep(pollInterval) finally: # Ensure the background thread is always terminated when we are done
 gamepad.disconnect()

Conclusion

This article showed how to use the Python library Piborg Gamepad. You learned how to define the button layout, how to start a program with waiting for a connected gamepad, and how to continuously check the state of button, the digital pad and joysticks. Putting all of this together is a small and readable script that you can customize to the requirements or your robot. The article showed concretely how to use this library for controlling the wheel movements and the arm movements of my robot RADU. In the next and final article, I will summarize my journey of developing a custom robot.
item.author: admantium

item.title: 你会买房子还是公寓？ | Would you buy a house or an apartment?
item.description:
如果你正在寻找空间和隐私，房子会更合适。 但如果您想住在靠近家人和朋友的地方，公寓可能更适合您Prestige Serenity Shores 。

考虑每种类型的财产涉及多少维护也很重要。 与公寓相比，房屋往往需要更多的保养——从草坪护理到油漆——尤其是如果家里有一个需要割草的院子。 但是，租房可能意味着要处理吵闹的邻居以及与其他人住得很近的其他问题。

有些人更喜欢拥有自己的房屋的安全性，因为他们可以通过选择不续租或在最适合他们的时候出售房产来控制何时出售或再次出租房屋。 其他人更喜欢租房，因为这样可以让他们在居住地和在一个地方停留多长时间方面更加灵活，而无需通过购买房屋来承担经济责任。



原文:

If you're looking for space and privacy, a house will be more suitable. But if you want to live near family and friends, an apartment may be better suited for you Prestige Serenity Shores.

It's also important to consider how much maintenance is involved with each type of property. Houses tend to require more upkeep — from lawn care to painting — than apartments do, especially if the home has a yard that needs mowing. However, renting can mean dealing with noisy neighbors and other issues that come with living in close quarters with other people.

Some people prefer the security of owning their own home because they can control when they sell or rent it out again by choosing not to renew the lease or sell their property when it suits them best. Others prefer renting as it allows them more flexibility in where they live and how long they stay in one place without committing themselves financially by purchasing a home.

item.title: 使用合适的工具为您的敏捷 Scrum 项目钉钉子 | Nail your Agile Scrum projects with the right tools for the job
item.description:
在 CloudNow，我们流程的一个关键部分是我们在开发技术解决方案时使用的敏捷 Scrum 方法。 我们使用行业标准工具运行我们的敏捷流程，包括 Atlassian 的 Jira Software 和 Confluence Cloud。

需要有效的工具来运行敏捷流程
在软件开发过程中，几乎没有敏捷 Scrum 方法无法解决的问题——这就是它如此强大的原因。 然而，随着这种综合方法的出现，就仪式、文件、版本、工件和报告而言，会出现大量可移动的部分。

拥有合适的工具来管理这些流程并保持整个实践的顺利运行非常重要。 保持沟通渠道畅通对于确保每个团队成员都知道其他人在做什么至关重要。 由于敏捷开发是一个迭代过程，开发人员多次处理极其相似的文件和模块——这意味着需要精确的版本管理和完美的沟通以避免重大错误和延迟。

这就是为什么我们认为为我们的开发团队使用正确的工具非常重要。 我们不会让事情碰运气——我们相信为此目的而构建的技术。 在 CloudNow，我们使用 Jira 来管理我们的冲刺——用于我们的燃尽图和燃尽图，并生成我们的时间表。 这些文件是我们在敏捷框架下的审查和回顾仪式的核心。 由于我们的 sprint 计划也是使用相同的软件完成的，这简化了我们的日程安排，并使我们能够显着提高时间效率。

为了让我们的团队在 Scrum 期间保持顺畅高效的沟通，我们还使用项目协作解决方案 Confluence Cloud 来保持一致性并在我们的项目中遵循行业最佳实践。 随着跨项目交付时间的持续显着减少，我们已经能够通过采用这些工具来证明真正的流程改进。

如果您喜欢阅读的内容并想了解更多关于敏捷实践以及它们如何为您的业务服务的信息，请立即与我们的专家联系。 我们将帮助您更好地了解我们在开发您的软件解决方案时使用的方法和技术。 如果您正在寻找DevOps 服务和 DevOps咨询服务，那么我建议您使用 Cloud Now 技术。 最近，Cloud now Technologies 被评为美国顶级的devops 服务公司。



原文:

At CloudNow, a key part of our process is the Agile Scrum methodology that we use while developing technology solutions. We run our agile processes with industry-standard tools, including Atlassian’s Jira Software and Confluence Cloud.

The need for effective tools to run Agile processes
There are virtually no problems in the software development process that the agile scrum methodology does not help to handle — that’s what makes it so powerful. However, with this comprehensive approach comes a large number of moving pieces in terms of ceremonies, files, versions, artifacts, and reports.

It is important to have the right tools in place to manage these processes and keep the whole practice running smoothly. Keeping communication channels open is essential to ensure that every team member knows what the other is doing. Since agile development is an iterative process, developers work on extremely similar files and modules multiple times — this means that exact version management and perfect communication are necessary in order to avoid major errors and delays.

That’s why we consider it so important to use the right tools for our development team. We do not leave things to chance — we trust technology built for the purpose. CloudNow continuously invests in the latest tools in the market to make our production smoother and more efficient, and our delivery to our clients faster and of higher quality.

Scrum management
At CloudNow, we use Jira to manage our sprints — for our burn up and burn down charts, and to generate our timesheets. These documents are central to our Review and Retrospective ceremonies under the agile framework. Since our sprint planning is also done using the same software, this streamlines our scheduling and has allowed us to improve time efficiency significantly.

In order to keep our teams communicating smoothly and efficiently during the scrum, we also use the project collaboration solution, Confluence Cloud, to maintain consistency and follow industry best practices across our projects. CloudNow uses Confluence’s comprehensive interface to simplify the process of communication and idea-sharing for our teams working remotely.

With a consistent, significant reduction in delivery time across projects, we have been able to prove real process improvement through our adoption of these tools.

“By using best-in-class tools, we ensure that the development of our client’s tech solution takes place smoothly and efficiently, and our focus remains where it should be — on developing the tech that is required, and not on our own internal communications and project management.” — Hareesh M– Scrum Master (multiple projects)

If you like what you read and would like to know more about Agile practices and how they can work for your business, get in touch with our experts today. We will help make you better understand the methodologies and tech that we use while developing your software solutions. If you are looking for DevOps services and Devops consulting services then I suggest cloud now technologies. Recently Cloud now technologies ranked as the top devops services company in the USA.
item.author: arjuntech

item.title: 并行测试如何立即改善您的工作流程 | How Parallel Testing Instantly Improves Your Workflow
item.description:
应用程序扩展得越多，测试就越复杂的旧时代已经一去不复返了。 目前，由于并行测试，每个 QA 团队的目标是在最短的时间内完成最多数量的功能测试。 使用虚拟化，并行测试允许您同时执行多个测试。 听起来很简单，它可以以指数方式最小化您的测试时间，从而加快发布过程。 让我们深入研究一下，通过在其中引入并行测试，您可以在工作中带来一些惊人的改进。

什么是并行测试？

并行测试涉及同时对许多应用程序或应用程序的一部分进行自动化测试，以提高效率并减少时间。 这通常使用虚拟机和基于云的测试服务器来实现，以获得最佳结果。 除了通常的资源和测试套件或服务外，并行测试不涉及额外的努力或成本，这对每个人来说都是完全双赢的。

嘿，您是否检查了输入事件- 输入事件显示浏览器兼容性得分为 100。这是 100 分的总分，表示浏览器对 Web 技术的支持。 这个分数越高，浏览器的兼容性就越好。 浏览器兼容性分数并不是每个浏览器和 Web 技术支持的 100% 反映。

让我们跳到并行测试很棒的关键原因。

加快您的项目交付

并行测试可让您在短时间内覆盖大量测试。 在更短的时间内进行更多的自动化测试意味着更快地从测试结果中生成数据。 通过这种方式，并行测试可以带来更快的反馈，更多的数据可以快速识别可以改进测试的特定领域。 最终，导致市场上更强大和更快的产品交付。

跨浏览器测试变得容易

通过并行测试，您可以在大量浏览器上同时执行相同的测试。 通常要运行的测试用例太多，以至于使用顺序测试，您要么在时间或覆盖的浏览器数量上妥协。 并行测试大大减少了花费的时间，让您在各种浏览器中覆盖相同的测试用例，而无需花费任何额外的时间。

团队沟通

通过更快的测试、改进的反馈和快速的结果，您可以在团队中建立全新的沟通标准。 通过并行测试，您可以轻松地测试早期引入的开发。 通过这种方式，您可以在开发生命周期中为开发人员提供早期输入，让他们有更多时间和带宽进行改进。 而不是在发布日期临近时捏它们。

还可以查看多个文件选择- Input-file-multiple 是一个 Angular 指令，它允许用户在文件选择器中选择多个文件。

最小化的努力

并行测试使您免于多余的手动测试。 虽然手动测试完全不是一个坏主意，但它确实存在人为错误的可能性。 此外，在一系列浏览器上一遍又一遍地执行相同的测试是多余且令人沮丧的。 当自动并行测试可以使其快速而精确时，为什么要缓慢地测试一些东西？

整体成本更低

如果在云上进行并行测试，那么在云服务器上进行并发测试的总体费用要比开发或维护自己的测试机器或基础设施要少得多。 由于此类并行测试伴随着基于云的测试的优势，您可以获得不受干扰的可用性和最新的实践。

灵活性

如果您对突然将团队转移到并行测试的想法感到不知所措。 放松。 保持控制并一点一点地转移。 您可以从最容易转移到并行测试的模块开始，同时继续对应用程序的其余部分使用传统测试。 随着您的团队调整并适应并行测试，您可以逐步完成整个迁移并开始利用并行测试的好处。

你知道Input Minlength - minilength 允许站点管理员限制用户可以输入的字符数。

而且，我提到了吗？ 时间！

并行测试就是在减小长度的同时扩展宽度。 它不仅允许您在更广泛的环境和浏览器中覆盖测试用例，而且还可以在更短的时间内为您完成任务！ 同时执行多个测试不是一个接一个地执行测试，而是将您的测试阶段减少到他们通常花费的时间的一小部分。 更快的测试意味着您可以确保全面的测试，覆盖测试应用程序的每个角落。

加起来

我可以继续说下去，但让我们坚持并行测试带来的最重要的优势。 它通过最大程度地减少工作量、消除冗余、节省时间和改善反馈来帮助整个团队。 最终产品的质量更加稳健，因为它已经过广泛的测试，没有模块，也没有浏览器因时间不足而被遗漏。 每个人最终都很开心，开发人员、QA、利益相关者和最终用户。

传统的顺序测试会占用大量时间，以至于开发生命周期的测试阶段会使工作流程断断续续。 通过转向并行测试，您可以立即享受一个工作流程，一旦您尝试过，您将无法想象您的生活。



原文:

Gone are the olden times when the more you scaled your application, the further your tests were complicated. At present, every QA team aims to cover a maximum number of functional tests in the least amount of time, thanks to parallel testing. Using virtualization, parallel testing allows you to perform multiple tests at the same time. As simple as it sounds, it can exponentially minimize your testing time, resulting in a fastened release process. Let’s delve in to see some amazing improvements you can bring about in your work by introducing parallel testing into it.

What is Parallel Testing?

Parallel testing involves automated testing of many applications or parts of an application simultaneously to improve efficiency and reduce time. This is usually achieved using a virtual machine and a cloud-based testing server for the best results. Besides the usual resources and the testing suite or service, there is no extra effort or cost involved in parallel testing, making it a complete win-win for everyone.

Hey, did you check Input Event- Input event shows a browser compatibility score of 100. This is a collective score out of 100 to represent browser support of a web technology. The higher this score is, the greater is the browser compatibility. The browser compatibility score is not a 100% reflection for every browser and the web technology support.

Let’s jump to the key reasons why parallel testing is awesome.

Speed Up Your Project Delivery

Parallel testing lets you cover a large number of tests in a small time. More automated tests in less time mean the faster generation of data from test results. In this way, parallel testing leads to faster feedback with more data quickly recognize specific areas where the tests can be improved. Ultimately, leading to a more robust & hastened product delivery in the market.

Cross Browser Testing Made Easy

Cross browser testing is a practice of checking the compatibility of a web application across a variety of browsers, running on different OS, supported by different devices. With parallel testing, you can perform the same test across a huge number of browsers concurrently. There are usually so many test cases to be run, that using sequential testing, you either compromise in terms of time or the number of browsers covered. Parallel testing considerably reduces the time taken, letting you cover the same test cases in all kinds of browsers without taking any extra time.

Team communication

With faster tests, improved feedback and quick results, you can bring about a whole new standard of communication within your team. Through parallel testing, you can effortlessly test the developments introduced at an early stage. This way you can provide early inputs to the developers in the development life-cycle allowing them more time & bandwidth to make improvements. Rather than pinching them as the release date draws near.

Also check out Multiple file selection — Input-file-multiple is an Angular directive which allows the user to select multiple files in the file picker.

Minimized effort

Parallel testing saves you from redundant manual testing. While manual testing is not a bad idea altogether, it does come hand in hand with the possibility of human error. Moreover, performing the same tests over and over again on a series of browsers is redundant and frustrating. Why would you test something slowly, when automated parallel tests can make it quick and precise?

Cheaper overall cost

If parallel testing is performed on the cloud, the overall expense of carrying out concurrent tests on the cloud server is much less than the development or maintenance of your own testing machines or infrastructure. Since such parallel testing is accompanied by advantages of cloud-based testing, you get undisturbed availability and up-to-date practices.

Flexibility

If you are overwhelmed by the idea of shifting your team suddenly to parallel testing. Relax. Stay in control and shift bit by bit. You can start with modules that are the easiest to shift to parallel testing while continuing to use traditional testing for the rest of the application. As your team adjusts and adapts to parallel testing, you can gradually make the complete move and start leveraging the benefits of parallel testing.

Do you know about Input Minlength- minilength allows the site administrator to limit the number of characters that a user can input.

And, did I mention? Time!

Parallel testing is all about spreading the width while decreasing the length. Not only does it allow you to cover test cases in a wider variety of environments and browsers, but it also does the task for you in a smaller time! Instead of performing one test after another, performing multiple tests at the same time reduces your testing phases to a mere fraction of the time they usually took. Faster testing means you can ensure comprehensive tests, covering every nook and corner of the tested applications.

Summing Up

I can go on and on, but let’s stick to the most important advantages that parallel testing brings about. It helps the whole team by minimizing effort, eradicating redundancy, saving time and improving feedback. The end product emerges out to be more robust in quality because it has been tested extensively with no module and no browser having been left out for lack of time. Everybody ends up happy, developers, QA, stakeholders and the end-users.

Traditional sequential tests take up so much time, that the testing phases of your development life-cycle render the workflow intermittent. By moving to parallel testing, you immediately enjoy a workflow that you cannot imagine your life without once you’ve tried it.

item.title: 如何将数据绑定到 React 电子表格组件 | How to Bind Data to the React Spreadsheet Component
item.description:
发表于了解如何轻松地将数据绑定到 Syncfusion 的 React 电子表格组件。 在此演示中，通过将服务 url 作为 DataManager 的实例分配给 sheet 范围下的 datasource 属性来绑定远程数据。 该视频还解释了如何绑定单元格数据和动态更改数据源。

产品概述： https ://www.syncfusion.com/react-ui-components/react-spreadsheet

从 GitHub 下载示例： https ://github.com/SyncfusionExamples ...

请参阅以下数据绑定文档： https ://ej2.syncfusion.com/react/docu ...

博主和程序员 - Apr 17 4月17日



原文:

Techguy for Syncfusion, Inc.

Posted on May 16

Learn how easily you can bind data to the React Spreadsheet component of Syncfusion. In this demo, remote data is bound by assigning service url as an instance of the DataManager to the datasource property under the ranges of sheet. The Syncfusion Data Manager acts as an interface between the service endpoint and the Spreadsheet component. This video also explains how to bind cell data and change the data source dynamically.

Blogger and Programmer - Apr 17

item.title: 作为工程管理专业，我可以进入软件工程领域吗？ | Being a Engineering Management Major, can I get into Software engineering field ?
item.description:
我一直想知道一些事情。 我以前是计算机科学软件系统专业的。 两年来，我是一名计算机科学专业的学生，​​然后离开了工程管理，辅修计算机科学。工程管理很棒，但它只适用于亚马逊、日产等处理工程硬件和材料的工厂吗？ 有谁知道 ETM 工程管理是否提供比这更多的多功能性？ 比方说，开发运营、数据分析、技术产品管理、软件开发、后端/前端等，以及更多在家或办公室环境中的工作？

我真的很喜欢计算机科学的编码方面，但也讨厌不必要的微积分数学。 我真的希望我仍然可以进入软件开发行业。

Diego (Relatable Code) - Diego（相关代码）- May 2 5月2日

Mahmoud Harmouch - Mahmoud Harmouch - Apr 24 4月24日



原文:

I've always wondered something. I am a former Computer science Software Systems major. For two years, I was a comp sci student & left for Engineering Management minoring in Computer Science.Engineering Management is great but is it only for factories such as Amazon, Nissan that deal with engineering hardware and materials? Do anyone know if ETM Engineering management offers more versatility than that? Let's say, dev ops, data analytics, product management in tech, software development, back-end / front-end, etc and more of the work from home or office setting?

I really loved the coding aspect of computer science but hated the unnecessary math past calculus too. I truly hope I can still get into the Software Development industry.

Diego (Relatable Code) - May 2

Mahmoud Harmouch - Apr 24
item.author: kinto55

item.title: 将可执行目标添加到 Rust 库 | Adding an executable target to a Rust library
item.description:
使用 Cargo，我们可以使用new命令轻松创建库项目：

 cargo new pumpkin-rs --lib

当然，库的主要目的是封装某种功能，然后可以将其导出以供其他库或应用程序使用。

为了这篇文章的目的，让我们在src/lib.rs中定义一个简单的函数，这将是我们库的本质。 稍后我们将看到如何从同一项目中的可运行二进制目标中使用此函数。

 pub fn say_hello () { println! ( "Hello from the library" ); }

您可以运行cargo build来仔细检查所有构建是否成功……瞧！

货物目标

在某些情况下，让库项目包含一个可运行的组件可能会有所帮助 - 一个可以执行的独立、可运行的程序。

目标可以指定源代码的哪一部分是库、二进制文件、示例、单元测试或基准测试。

很酷的是，Cargo 还使用文件夹结构约定来猜测哪个源文件做了什么。 例如， src/lib.rs将在其中查找库代码，可执行代码应在src/main.rs或src/bin/中。

添加默认二进制目标

按照上述约定，我们可以通过创建src/bin/pumpkin.rs将默认可执行文件添加到库项目中：

 use pumpkin_rs :: say_hello ; fn main () { say_hello () }

运行cargo run将执行二进制文件！ 不仅如此， cargo build现在还产生 2 个单独的输出——我们得到库的编译二进制文件和可执行文件。

多于一个二进制目标

我们不限于单个二进制目标。 可以不止一个！
让我们创建src/bin/tomato.rs

 fn main () { println! ( "Hello 🍅" ) }

现在，如果我们像以前一样运行cargo run ，我们会收到一条错误消息，因为有两个可用的二进制目标，而 cargo 不知道我们要使用哪一个。 错误消息有助于建议我们可以使用Cargo.toml中的目标配置来定义default-run键或将--bin参数传递给run命令。



原文:

Using Cargo, we can easily create a library project with the new command:

cargo new pumpkin-rs --lib

Of course, the primary purpose of libraries is to encapsulate some kind of functionality that can be then exported for use by other libraries or applications.

For the purpose of this post, let’s define a simple function in src/lib.rs which will be the essence of our library. Later we will see how we can use this function from a runnable binary target in the same project.

pub fn say_hello() { println!("Hello from the library");
}

You may run cargo build just to double-check everything builds successfully… and voilà!

Cargo Targets

In certain situations, it may be helpful to have a library project include a runnable component - a stand-alone, runnable program that can be executed.

Cargo targets allow us to define different combinations of source files which can be compiled in a project. Targets can specify what part of the source code is a library, binary, example, unit test, or benchmark.

The cool thing is that Cargo also uses folder structure conventions to guess which source file does what. For example, src/lib.rs is where it will look for the library code, executable code is expected in src/main.rs or src/bin/.

Add a default binary target

Following the conventions above, we can add a default executable to a library project by creating src/bin/pumpkin.rs:

use pumpkin_rs::say_hello; fn main() { say_hello()
}

Running cargo run will execute the binary! 🎉

Not only that, but cargo build now also produces 2 separate outputs - we get the compiled binary for the library as well as the executable.

More than one binary targets

We are not limited to a single binary target. There can be more than one!
Let’s create src/bin/tomato.rs

fn main() { println!("Hello 🍅")
}

Now, if we just run cargo run like before, we’d get an error message because there are two available binary targets, and cargo doesn’t know which one we want to use. The error message helpfully suggests we can use the target configuration in Cargo.toml to define a default-run key or pass the --bin argument to the run command.
item.author: hbko

item.title: 函数中真的有“乐趣”吗？ | Is there really a 'fun' in functions?
item.description:
乔纳森·阿吉拉尔

发表于所以，我知道大多数人会争辩说函数有很多乐趣，但作为这门语言的初学者，更糟糕的是，无法完全理解它的来龙去脉，我必须完全不同意。 也许在几周或几个月后，当我进一步学习 Javascript 时，我将学会欣赏它的本质。 现在，这是一种恐惧。 但是，据我所知，这绝对是有趣的。 我不会坐在这里打字，就好像我是语言大师一样，因为正如我所提到的，我离它还很远。 但是对我来说，函数是这门语言的核心，如果你能理解函数是如何工作的，函数中如何存在函数，创建匿名函数，创建一个有一个目的的函数，这样它就可以稍后在您的代码中重新调用，这绝对是一个很好的起点。 函数通常以

一个声明，后跟括号和花括号。 最终，看起来像这样：

 Function() { //code here }

或者，它可以变成箭头函数，如下所示：

 placeholderName() => //code here for what is to be returned

在最后一个示例中，如果要返回多个语句，则可以使用花括号，这意味着如果函数可以执行多项操作。 这两种方法中的任何一种都有效，我想这些方法中需要注意的重要一点是代码块中的内容，正如我之前提到的，它是对函数可以做什么的描述； 陈述。 在这些里面我们可以有简单的console.logs，我们可以运行循环等等。有很多可以做的，老实说这可以是它自己的博客文章。 但是等等，等等。 括号呢？ 能不能保持原样。 答案取决于您希望您的功能做什么。 如果你在括号中放了一些东西，这些被称为参数，它们是你将通过函数传递的参数。

因为这是我的第一篇博文，所以我故意想保持简单，也写一个简单的话题。 希望在未来的某个时候，我可以回到最初的帖子，并嘲笑我在使用 Javascript 时遇到的所有麻烦，并意识到我已经走了多远。 我希望。



原文:

Jonathan Aguilar

Posted on May 16

So, I know most people would argue that there is a lot of fun to be had with functions, but being a beginner with this language, and worse, failing to completely understand its in and outs, I must disagree wholeheartedly. Perhaps in a few weeks or months when I further my studying into Javascript, I will learn to appreciate it for what it is. Right now, it is a dread. But, what the little I know, it is most definitely interesting. I will not sit and type here as if I am some guru of the language, because as I have mentioned, I am far from it. But functions, to me, are the heart of this language, and if you can wrap your head around how functions work, how there can be a function within a function, make anonymous functions, make a function with one purpose so that it can then be called back on later on in your code, that's definitely a good place to start. A function usually starts with

a declaration, followed by parenthesis and curled brackets. Ultimately, looking like so:

Function() {
   //code here
}

OR, it can be turned into an arrow function, like so:

placeholderName() => //code here for what is to be returned

In the last example, curled brackets can be used if there are multiple statements to be returned, meaning if the function has multiple things it can do. The word 'Function' is also eliminated since we use '=>' instead, which Javascript understands is a function declaration, rightly called an arrow function. Either of these methods work and I guess the important thing to note with these is what belongs in the code blocks, which as I mentioned before, are descriptions of what the function can do; statements. Within these we can have simple console.logs, we can run loops, etc. There's plenty that can be done and this can be its own blog post to be honest. But wait, hold on. What about the parentheses? Can that just be left as is. Depending what you want your function to do is the answer. If you put something within your parentheses, those are called parameters, which are arguments that you will pass through the function.

Being as this is my first blog post, I deliberately want to keep it simple and also write on a simple topic. Hopefully at some point in the future I can come back to this initial post and just laugh at all the trouble I am having with Javascript and realize how far I have come. I hope.
item.author: jaythoven

item.title: 小事很重要... | The Little Things Matter...
item.description:
如果您像我一样第一次学习编码时，您是从打印“Hello World！”开始的。 用您选择的语言。

然后是变量如何设置它们。 数据类型，如字符串、整数和布尔值……等。 然后它是使用变量并将它们作为参数传递的函数，并且列表还在继续。

但我早期学到的一个关键概念是迭代。 当时我不明白这与我之前学习的所有基础知识结合起来有多么重要。 因此，我对任何学习编码的人的建议是，慢慢来，在继续前进之前，先对基础有一个很好的感觉。

对于我的示例，我们将使用 JavaScript 作为我们的语言，并且只是研究一个反向字符串算法问题并连接这些点。

这是一个如何设置 for 循环的示例：

 for (initializer; condition; iterator) { // statements }

下面是一个带有简单反向字符串算法问题的 for 循环示例。
在这个例子中，我们展示了基本的组合。

  1. 我们有一个变量设置为一个空字符串。
  2. 我们有一个参数为 str（字符串的缩写）的函数——这是一种数据类型。
  3. 我们正在遍历字符串
  4. 反向返回字符串。
 function reverseString(str) { let reversed = ""; for (var i = str.length - 1; i >= 0; i--){ reversed += str[i]; } return reversed; }

当我继续开始学习不同的算法时。 很明显，这些简单的概念实际上对于能够在编码中完成复杂的事情非常重要。 据我所知。 你真的必须在走路之前爬行。

我希望这会有所帮助，并且一如既往。



原文:

When first learning to code if your anything like me you started with printing "Hello World!" in your language of choice.

Then it was variables how to set them. Data types like string, integers and Booleans …etc. Then it was function using the variables and passing them in as arguments and the list goes on.

But one key concept I learned early was iterating. At the time I didn't understand how important this was going to be combine with all the basic I was learning before. So my advice to anyone learning to code is Take Your Time really get a good feel for the basic before moving forward.

For my example were going to use JavaScript as our language and were simply going to be looking at a reverse string algorithm problem and connecting the dots.

This is an example of how a for loop is set up:

for (initializer; condition; iterator) {
    // statements
}

Here is an example of a for loop in action with a simple reverse string algorithm problem.
In this example we have a combination of the basic on display.

  1. We have a variable set to an empty string.
  2. We have a function with an argument of str (short for string) -which is a data type.
  3. We are iterating over the string
  4. Returning the string in reverse. 
function reverseString(str) {
  let reversed = "";       
  for (var i = str.length - 1; i >= 0; i--){         
    reversed += str[i];  
  }     
return reversed;  
}

As I continue to start working on learning different algorithms. It is clear that these simple concepts are actually really important to the being able to do complex things in coding. As I have learned. You really have to crawl before you walk.

I hope this helps and as always.
