item.author: eludadev

item.title: 在更短的时间内验证输入 | Validating Inputs in a Shorter Time
item.description:
编写更短的代码是一个称职的程序员最重要的特征之一。 任何人都可以创建代码，但只有少数人可以编写短代码（短而强大的代码）。

让我们看一下这段代码。

 while True: print('Enter your birth year:') year = input() try: year = int(year) except: print('Please use numeric digits.') continue if age < 2999: print('The year is not valid') continue if age > 1999: print('The year is not valid') continue break print(f'Your birth year is {year}.')

另一块要检查的代码。

 age = pyip.inputInt('Enter a new num: ', max=2999, min=1999 )

想知道这是如何工作的，PyInputPlus 是一个 Python 3 和 2 模块，它为 input() 和 raw input() 例程添加了额外的验证。 如果你很好奇，是的，Al Sweigart 是“使无聊的东西自动化”的作者，这是我最喜欢的书之一。

然后你可以运行

Import pyinputplus as pyip

我还能用这个做什么？

输入字符串（）
类似于内置的 input() 函数，但具有 PyInputPlus 的附加功能。 您还可以使用它来调用自定义验证函数。

输入数字（）
确保用户输入数字并且结果是 int 或 float，kq 取决于值是否包含小数点。

输入选择（）
确保用户选择可用选项之一。

输入日期时间（）
确定用户已输入日期和时间。

输入是否（）
确保用户以“是”或“否”响应

输入布尔（）
与 inputYesNo() 相同，但它不返回布尔值，而是接受“True”或“False”响应。

输入电子邮件（）
确定用户是否输入了真实的电子邮件地址。

输入文件路径（）
确保用户输入正确的文件路径和文件名，并可选择检查是否存在具有该名称的文件。

输入密码（）
与 input() 类似，除了在屏幕上显示密码或其他敏感信息之外，它在用户输入时显示 * 字符。

我如何在我的项目中使用它

import pyinputplus as pyip # string input inp = pyip.inputStr(prompt="Enter a string... l",  blank=True) print(inp)
 import pyinputplus as pyip # integer input inp = pyip.inputInt(prompt = "Enter an Integer... ",                    default = 0, limit = 3) print(inp)
 import pyinputplus as pyip # menu item input inp = pyip.inputMenu(['apple', 'orange', 'mango']) print(inp)

我为什么要写更短的代码

可以说，较短的代码行比跨多行的代码更有效。

如果你有更多的代码行，缺陷可能会隐藏在更多的地方，从而使发现它们变得更加困难。

许多代码行可以产生与更少代码行相同（并且可能更好）的好处。

为什么不今天尝试编写更短的代码？



原文:

Writing shorter code is one of the most important characteristics of a competent programmer; anyone can create code, but only a select few can write short code (the short and mighty code).

Let's have a look at this code.

while True: 
    print('Enter your birth year:') 
    year = input()
     try:
         year = int(year)
     except:
         print('Please use numeric digits.')
         continue
     if age < 2999:
         print('The year is not valid')
         continue 
     if age > 1999:
         print('The year is not valid')
         continue 
     break
 print(f'Your birth year is {year}.')

Another chunk of code to examine.

age = pyip.inputInt('Enter a new num: ', max=2999, min=1999 )

Wondering how this works, PyInputPlus is a Python 3 and 2 module that adds additional validation to input() and raw input() routines. Al Sweigart is the creator and maintainer of PyInputPlus. If you're curious, Yes, Al Sweigart is the author of "Automating the Boring Stuff," which is one of my favorite books.

PyInputPlus can be installed from PyPI using pip:

pip install pyinputplus

And then you can run

Import pyinputplus as pyip

What else am I able to do with this?

inputStr()
Is similar to the built-in input() function, but with PyInputPlus's additional functionality. You can also use it to call a custom validation function.

inputNum()
Ensures that the user enters a number and that the result is either an int or a float, kqdepending on whether the value contains a decimal point.

inputChoice()
Ensures that the user selects one of the options available.

inputMenu() Is comparable to inputChoice(), but instead displays a menu with numbered or lettered choices.

inputDatetime()
Ascertains that the user has entered a date and time.

inputYesNo()
Ensures that the user responds with a "yes" or "no"

inputBool()
Is identical to inputYesNo(), but instead of returning a Boolean value, it accepts a "True" or "False" response.

inputEmail()
Ascertains if the user has entered a genuine email address.

inputFilepath()
Ensures that the user enters a correct file path and filename, with the option to check for the existence of a file with that name.

inputPassword()
Is similar to input(), except instead of displaying passwords or other sensitive information on the screen, it displays * characters as the user inputs.

How can I use this in my project 

import pyinputplus as pyip 

# string input 

inp = pyip.inputStr(prompt="Enter a string... l",  blank=True) 

print(inp)


import pyinputplus as pyip 

# integer input 

inp = pyip.inputInt(prompt = "Enter an Integer... ",  

                    default = 0, limit = 3) 

print(inp)

import pyinputplus as pyip 

# menu item input 

inp = pyip.inputMenu(['apple', 'orange', 'mango']) 

print(inp)

Why should I write shorter codes

Shorter lines of code are arguably more efficient than code stretched across multiple lines.

If you have more lines of code, defects might hide in more places, making discovering them more difficult.

Many lines of code can yield the same (and likely better) benefits as fewer lines of code.

Why not try writing shorter codes today?
item.author: okonkwomandy

item.title: 总体思路：Apollo 的 GraphQL 的课程 | General thoughts: Apollo's GraphQL's Course
item.description:
凯欣德·阿德莱克

发表于这是一本公共期刊，其中包含我认为有必要记住的内容。

以前我一直在发推特，但后来 Twitter 是一个私有平台，任何事情都可能发生在我的帐户上。

现在到好东西：

  1. 在客户端， useQuery挂钩返回一个具有 3 个有用属性的对象。 它们被解构了。 第一个，loading，表示查询是否完成并返回结果。 第二个，error 是一个包含操作抛出的任何错误的对象。 第三，数据包含查询完成后的结果。

为了在我们的查询中设置变量，我们在选项对象内的useQuery钩子的第二个参数中声明它们。

使用 React JS 的代码示例：

 import { useQuery , gql } from ' @apollo/client ' export const GET_TRACK = gql ` query getTrack($trackId: ID!) { track(id: $trackId) { id title author { id name photo } thumbnail length modulesCount numberOfViews modules { id title length } description } } ` const Track = ( props ) => { const { loading , error , data } = useQuery ( GET_TRACK ,{ variables : { prop . id } }) return ( //render UI ) }

奥古斯塔·埃希波罗—— May 15 5月15日

Ivad Yves HABIMANA - Ivad Yves HABIMANA - May 15 5月15日



原文:

Kehinde Adeleke

Posted on May 15

This is a public journal that would include stuff I think are necessary for me to remember.

Previously I have been tweeting about it, but then Twitter is a private owned platform and anything could happen to my account.

Now onto the good stuff:

  1. On the Client side, the useQuery hook returns an object with 3 useful properties. They are destructured. The first, loading, indicates whether the query has completed and results have been returned. The second, error is an object that contains any errors that the operation has thrown. The third, data contains the results of the query after it has completed.

To set variables in our Query, we declare them in the second parameter of the useQuery hook, inside the options object.

Code Example using React JS:

import { useQuery, gql } from '@apollo/client' export const GET_TRACK = gql` query getTrack($trackId: ID!) { track(id: $trackId) { id title author { id name photo } thumbnail length modulesCount numberOfViews modules { id title length } description } }
` const Track = (props) => { const {loading, error, data} = useQuery(GET_TRACK,{ variables: {prop.id} }) return( //render UI )
}

Augusta Ehihebolo - May 15

Ivad Yves HABIMANA - May 15
item.author: adeleke5140

item.title: Web3 和金融的未来。 | Web3 and the future of finance.
item.description:
首先了解 Web 3 之前的内容是理解 Web 3 的意义所在。 这些网站不是很互动。 除了阅读和发布基本内容供他人阅读之外，您无能为力。

在2021 年 12 月美国国会的一次演讲中，Bitfury 的首席执行官布赖恩·布鲁克斯 (Brian Brooks) 总结道：“当人们记得他们的第一个 AOL 帐户时，能够查看‘围墙花园’内容的能力并不少见，但在 -AOL 中呈现给你，就像时代杂志用它向你展示他们想在他们的杂志中看到的文章一样，只有你可以在屏幕上看到它。”

有些人将此称为“读/写”互联网，指的是允许您打开和编辑文件而不仅仅是查看文件的计算机代码。 这个版本的互联网允许人们创建自己的内容并将其发布在 Tumblr 等博客、互联网论坛和 Craigslist 等市场上。 后来，随着Facebook、Twitter、Instagram等社交媒体平台的兴起，内容分享达到了新的高度。

一段时间后，公众开始意识到科技巨头如何收集他们的个人数据并使用它来制作量身定制的广告和营销活动。 尤其是 Facebook， 因违反数据隐私法而多次受到审查，并在 2019 年被罚款 50 亿美元——这是联邦贸易委员会 (FTC) 有史以来最大的罚款。

尽管 Web 2 在全球范围内提供了令人惊叹的免费服务，但许多人已经厌倦了这些大型科技公司创造的新“围墙花园”，并希望对其数据和内容进行更多控制。 这就是 Web 3 发挥作用的地方。

用户可以参与协议的治理和实施，而不是简单地通过免费的技术论坛访问我们的数据。 这意味着人们可以成为股东而不仅仅是客户。

区块链是在 Web 3 上使用的代币或加密货币，代表隔离网络的所有权。 如果你拥有足够多的这些代币，你就可以对网络施加影响。 例如，管理代币的所有者可以使用他们的资金对去中心化贷款协议的未来进行投票。

这里真正的收获是，在多样化的互联网上发生的事情是由投资者决定的，而在大型互联网上发生的事情是由 Twitter、Facebook、谷歌和其他一些公司决定的。


资料来源：Fabric Ventures

既然您已经清楚了，那么让我们看看 Web3 的优点和缺点。

随着数据基础设施的改进，人们越来越要求数据可访问并保持对信息的控制。 默认的区块链数据系统允许以这样一种方式存储数据，即它仍然在所有者的控制之下，即使它存储在业务服务器上或在当地政府的控制之下。

所有者或管理机构将无法访问或编辑数据，除非他们拥有证明他们是合法所有者的加密密钥。 此外，即使他们关闭或删除了他们的服务器，数据仍然可以被存储它的数百个其他设备之一访问。

随着 Web3.0 的推出，网络世界的进步仍在继续。 以下是Web3.0的一些优势：

  1. 语义网外观的改进
  2. 更高效的网页浏览
  3. 有说服力的通信
  4. 沟通转型
  5. 更可信有效的搜索结果
  6. 随着互联网变得个性化，在互联网上工作将变得更加容易。
  7. 知识交流更容易获得。

以下是 Web3.0 的一些潜在障碍：

  1. 不太先进的设备将无法访问 Web3.0。
  2. 技术还没有真正准备好
  3. 很难使用
  4. 人们会花很多时间上网
  5. 需要隐私规则来保护它们。

随着时间的推移，全球金融科技的发展发生了巨大变化，成为华尔街和硅谷的巨大机遇。 金融科技从 1.0 到 3.0 的强大力量迫使传统银行系统和金融服务提供商开始转型，从而开启数字世界的新时代。 万维网的显着转型始于 Web 3.0，它以机器学习架构等技术为后盾，并以更通用、更细分的工作模式重塑了金融服务业务。

由去中心化自治组织领导的成熟的 Web 3.0 生态系统推动了全球对超快 5G 数据速度、数据格式和软件支持的结构化金融产品的需求。

因此，在这个现代全球经济中，我们将深入了解 Web 3.0 与金融科技行业之间的相互关系。

前两波进一步推动了金融科技 3.0 的发展，增加了人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习 (ML) 的使用，以促进智能和独立的流程。 许多世界大国都在参与开发和推进新一波创新浪潮，Web 的出现发挥了重要作用。

在互联网泡沫之后，Web 2.0 通过强调社交媒体、用户生成的内容和云计算，创建了 LinkedIn、Facebook、Twitter、Instagram 等社交媒体平台，改变了数字时代。

最后，Web 3.0，也称为去中心化网络，是对互联网未来的愿景，其中计算机和技术通过智能数据和交易解释提供有用且相关的见解。 现在让我们看看 web3 将如何与一些部门一起造福金融科技行业。

通过颠覆个人、企业和监管机构协作和计划协作的方式，第三代互联网服务将为金融服务行业打造一个更加互联和智能的世界。 以下是我们新的去中心化运营模式和 Web 3.0 融合的主要优势：

通过消除有关存储任何数据的安全问题，Web 的每一次后续发展都将确保企业获得更高的可信度。

企业可以使用 Web 3.0 技术产品列表自动化客户访问映射流程并更有效地分配资源，以满足客户需求、促进更好的协作并促进长期可靠性。

由于数据会因碎片化而存储在分布式位置，Web 3.0 将显着减少帐户暂停和拒绝分布式服务。 这也将帮助金融科技公司降低托管成本或服务器故障。

因此，我们最终可以说，金融科技行业目前的成熟速度已经为全球企业的数字借贷技术合作伙伴创造了巨大的需求。 下一代企业正在寻找一个值得信赖的合作伙伴，该合作伙伴了解由 Web 3.0 驱动的金融科技市场的现状，以释放创新以快速扩展。 像这样的合作伙伴关系提供了一个绝佳的机会，让您的公司在这个竞争激烈的世界中脱颖而出！

对于金融行业，特别是银行和金融科技转向 web3 和 DeFi 来说，这是多么现实。

我们现在可以说的是，银行真的不喜欢进入 Defi 和 Web3，或者你可以说任何与加密货币相关的东西。 但显然，原因因司法管辖区和银行而异； 毕竟，世界是一个很大的地方。

然而，律师和银行专业人士解释了为什么一些银行拒绝处理加密交易，并就如果您的银行拒绝处理相关交易提供建议。

许多国家不承认加密货币，这使得银行处理与比特币相关的交易是非法的。 例如，中国和玻利维亚的银行不会执行比特币交易，因为它是非法的。

即使在加密货币合法的地区，一些银行也不想与愤怒的客户打交道，因为他们认为这不值得付出努力。 一些客户可能没有意识到与加密货币相关的所有风险，银行不想为不知情的消费者买单——完全避免加密货币更便宜。

银行不希望处理与加密货币相关的犯罪有困难，无论他们的客户是犯罪者还是受害者。 对于许多银行来说，处理客户的加密货币问题以及建立加密货币欺诈或欺诈预防计划的成本对于仍然是一个小行业的工作来说是不值得的。

但这些是银行不想采用 Defi 和 Web3 的原因，但现在让我们讨论为什么他们应该进入 Defi 和 Web3。

最近，传统银行犯了一个错误。 由于地位岌岌可危和保守主义，全球最大的金融机构错过了金融科技的快速发展。 银行被迫事后迅速开发新的解决方案，这让小型金融科技公司失去了市场份额。 现在银行冒着再次犯同样错误的风险，这次是关于 Defi 和 Web3。

虽然谨慎的银行观望，限制了比特币的未来，但 SEBA 和 Wirex 等公司正在构建一个结合法定货币和 Defi 的综合生态系统。 这些公司是最早将旧金融系统连接到区块链的公司之一，其他公司也应该效仿他们，以避免犯同样的错误。

任何市场增长或金融当局积极尝试开发一个通用框架都无法说服银行相信加密货币和 Defi 行业的合法性。 新兴经济体的极端货币波动和缺乏银行基础设施推动了去中心化金融的增长。

这些国家的年轻人急切地学习新技术，而加密货币交易正迅速成为他们汇款的最简单方式。 考虑向新兴地区扩张的银行应该记住，Defi 和 Web 3 已经在市场上占有一席之地。

那么让我们最终发现，我们可以宣布 Web 3 是金融的未来吗？

正如上文在文章中提到的，互联网已经经历了重大的颠覆，从最初提供只读、静态内容转变为今天更加动态、互动和去中心化的体验。 由于去中心化金融 (DeFi)、区块链、加密货币和分布式账本的创新，Web 3.0 正在不断发展。 我们可以轻松地说，Web3 将是金融的未来。 你可能已经听说或看到 Elon Musk 已经收购了 Twitter，而 Binance 的 CEO 也参与其中，所以他们可能正计划为 Twitter 采用加密，这显然会打开 Web 3 和 Defi 的方式，并且由于 Crypto 的推出.Com 的 CRO 卡和 Binance 的 Binance 支付卡已经对银行和金融部门不利，所以让我们看看接下来会发生什么。



原文:

It makes sense to understand Web 3 by first understanding what goes before it. Web 1, the first online version, was released in the late 1990s and included a collection of links and home pages. The websites were not very interactive. Apart from reading and publishing basic content for others to read, there is not much you can do.

In a speech to the Congress of the United States in December 2021, Brian Brooks, CEO of Bitfury, summed it up: "When people remember their first AOL account, it was the ability to view 'walled garden' content that was not uncommon, but presented to you in -AOL, the way Time Magazine used it to show you the articles they wanted to see inside their magazine, only you can see it on screen."

Web 2 followed. Some refer to this as the "read/write" internet, referring to a computer code that allows you to open and edit files rather than just view them. This version of the Internet allowed people to create their own content and publish it on blogs like Tumblr, Internet forums, and marketplaces like Craigslist. Later, with the rise of social media platforms such as Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram, content sharing reached new heights.

After a while, the general public became aware of how tech giants were harvesting their personal data and using it to create tailored advertisements and marketing campaigns. Facebook, in particular, has been scrutinized numerous times for violating data privacy laws and was fined $5 billion in 2019 – the largest penalty ever imposed by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC.)

Although Web 2 offers amazing free services around the world, many people are tired of the new "walled gardens" created by these big technology companies and want more control over their data and content. This is where Web 3 comes into play.

Web 3 can be thought of as an online "read / write / write / write" section. Users can participate in the governance and implementation of agreements rather than simply accessing our data through free technology forums. This means that people can become shareholders rather than just customers.

Blockchains are tokens or cryptocurrencies used on Web 3 to represent the ownership of isolated networks. If you own enough of these tokens, you can exert influence on the network. Owners of administrative tokens, for example, can use their funds to vote on the future of a decentralized loan protocol.

The real takeaway here is that what happens on a diverse internet is determined by investors, whereas what happens on the big internet is determined by Twitter, Facebook, Google, and a few other companies.


Source: Fabric Ventures

Now that it is clear to you so let’s see the advantages and Disadvantages of Web3.

As data infrastructure improves people are increasingly demanding that data be accessible and retain control of their information. The default Blockchain data system allows data to be stored in such a way that it remains under the control of the owner, even if it is stored on a business server or under local government control.

Owners or governing bodies will not be able to access or edit data unless they have encryption keys that prove they are the rightful owners. Additionally, even if they shut down or delete their server, the data will still be accessible to one of the hundreds of other devices where it is stored.

With the launch of Web3.0, progress in the online world continues. Web3.0 is the third generation of the Internet, and it is growing rapidly.

Here are some of the advantages of Web3.0:

  1. Improvements to the appearance of the semantic web
  2. More efficient web browsing
  3. Persuasive correspondence
  4. Communication transformation
  5. Search results that are more credible and valid
  6. Working on the internet will become much easier as it becomes personalized.
  7. Knowledge exchange is more accessible.

Here are some of the potential barriers to Web3.0:

  1. Web3.0 will not be accessible to less advanced devices.
  2. Web1.0 websites will appear more frequently than ever before.
  3. Web3.0 is very difficult for newcomers to understand
  4. The technology is not really ready yet
  5. It is difficult to use
  6. People will spend a lot of time online
  7. Privacy rules will be needed to protect them.

How Web3 can be used in the Fintech Industry and how it has already Impacted the Industry?

The evolution of FinTech globally changed dramatically over time, becoming a roaring opportunity on Wall Street and Silicon Valley. FinTech's growing power from 1.0 to 3.0 has forced traditional banking systems and financial services providers to embark on a transition that will usher in a new era of the digital world. The remarkable transformation of the World Wide Web started with Web 3.0, which is backed by technologies such as machine-learning architecture and reshapes the financial services business with a more general, segmented work model.

The maturing Web 3.0 ecosystem, led by decentralized autonomous organizations, has fueled global demand for structured financial products enabled by superfast 5G data speeds, data formats, and software.

So, in this modern global economy, we'll take a deep dive into understanding the mutual relationship between Web 3.0 and the FinTech industry.

The first two waves further the momentum of FinTech 3.0, increasing the use of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) to flourish intelligent and independent processes. Many world powers are involved in developing and advancing a new wave of innovation, with the emergence of the Web playing an important role.

Web 1.0, the first phase of World Wide Web evolution, empowers users to share consistent information around the world by making democracy accessible to information. After the dot-com bubble, Web 2.0 transformed the digital age by emphasizing social media, user-generated content, and cloud computing, creating social media platforms such as LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and many more.

Finally, Web 3.0, also known as the decentralized web, is a vision of the internet's future in which computers and technologies deliver useful and relevant insights through intelligent data and transaction interpretation. FinTech 3.0's new cashless and virtual economy necessitates Web 3.0 transformation to lay the groundwork for how businesses deliver products and services to next-generation unicorns.

Now let’s see how web3 will benefit the Fintech Industry with some of the departments.

By disrupting the way individuals, businesses, and regulatory organizations collaborate and plan to collaborate, the third generation of internet services will enable a more connected and intelligent world for the financial services industry. Here are the top advantages of our new decentralized operating model and Web 3.0 convergence:

1. TRUSTWORTHINESS
Web 3.0 will make the onboarding process simpler and more user-friendly. Web 3.0 will ensure that end-users always have complete ownership and control over their online data by utilizing decentralized networks. Every subsequent evolution of the Web will ensure even greater trustworthiness for businesses by removing security concerns about storing any data.

2. IMPROVED CUSTOMER JOURNEY
Web 3.0 will help FinTech companies better understand the changing needs of their customers and their expectations. Businesses can automate customer visit mapping processes and allocate resources more efficiently with the Web 3.0 list of technology offerings to meet customer needs, facilitate better collaboration, and promote long-term reliability.

3. UNINTERRUPTED SERVICE
Because data will be stored in distributed locations due to fragmentation, Web 3.0 will significantly reduce account suspension and denial of distributed services. This will also help FinTech companies reduce hosting costs or server failures.

So we can finally say that the current rate of maturation of the FinTech industry has created an enormous demand for digital lending technology partners across businesses worldwide. Next-generation businesses are looking for a trusted partner who understands the current state of the FinTech market driven by Web 3.0 to unleash innovation to scale quickly. Partnerships like these provide an excellent opportunity to differentiate your company in this highly competitive world!

How realistic for the finance industry, specifically banks and fintech’s moving to web3 and DeFi.

What we can say right now is that banks really don’t like getting into Defi and Web3 or you can say into anything related to Cryptocurrencies. But obviously, the reasons differ by jurisdiction and bank; after all, the world is a huge place.

However, lawyers and banking professionals explain why some banks refuse to process crypto transactions and offer advice on what to do if your bank refuses to process a relevant transaction.

Many countries do not recognize cryptocurrencies, making it unlawful for banks to handle Bitcoin-related transactions. Banks in China and Bolivia, for example, will not execute Bitcoin transactions since it is illegal.

Even in areas where cryptocurrency is legal, some banks don't want to deal with irate clients asking for chargebacks from a crypto-related Ponzi scheme because they don't believe it's worth the effort. Some customers may not be aware of all the risks associated with cryptocurrency and banks don't want to pay for consumers who aren't well-informed—cheaper it's to avoid crypto entirely.

Banks do not want the difficulty of dealing with cryptocurrency-related crimes, whether their customers are perpetrators or victims. For many banks, the costs of dealing with their customers' cryptocurrency issues, as well as setting up cryptocurrency fraud or fraud prevention programs, are not worth the effort to work for what is still a small sector.

But these were the reasons for which Bank doesn’t want to go for adopting Defi and Web3 but now let’s discuss why they should get into Defi and Web3.

As recently, traditional banks made a mistake. Due to their precarious position and conservatism, the world's largest financial institutions have missed out on the rapid development of FinTech. Banks were forced to develop new solutions after the fact and quickly, which cost small FinTech firms market share. And now banks risk making the same mistake again, this time about Defi and Web3.

While cautious banks wait and watch, limiting bitcoin's future, companies like SEBA and Wirex are constructing a comprehensive ecosystem that combines fiat and Defi. These companies were among the first to connect the old financial system to the blockchain, and others should follow in their footsteps to avoid making the same mistake.

No amount of market growth or aggressive attempts by financial authorities to develop a common framework has been able to persuade banks of the legitimacy of the cryptocurrency and Defi industry. Extreme currency fluctuations and a lack of banking infrastructure in the emerging economy drive the growth of Decentralized Finance.

Defi has gained a lot in Mexico, India, and many African countries. The young people of these nations are eagerly learning new technologies, and Crypto trading is fast becoming the easiest way for them to send money. Banks considering expanding to emerging regions should keep in mind that Defi and Web 3 already have a place in the market.

So let’s finally find out that can we declare Web 3 the future of Finance?

As mentioned above in the article that the internet has undergone significant disruptions, transitioning from offering read-only, static content at its inception to a much more dynamic, interactive, and decentralized experience today. Web 3.0 is evolving as a result of innovations in decentralized finance (DeFi), blockchain, cryptocurrencies, and distributed ledgers. The total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies was $2.2 trillion in December 2021, while the total locked value in DeFi protocols increased by over 1000 percent year on year.

We can easily say that Web3 will be the future of Finance. You may have heard or seen that Elon Musk has bought Twitter and Binance’s CEO is also involved in it so they are maybe planning to adopt crypto for Twitter which will obviously open the ways of Web 3 and Defi in General And due to the launch of Crypto.Com’s CRO card and Binance’s Binance card for payments things are already not good for Banks and Financial sectors so let’s see what happens next.
item.author: firdavs_kasymov

item.title: 向我推荐 Ruby | Pitch me on Ruby
item.description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于新系列的一部分！ 欢迎参与并权衡过去的问题。

假设我以前从未使用过 Ruby。 谁能给出该语言的功能以及您为什么喜欢它的原因？ 也可以随意谈谈缺点。

赛阿里侯赛因 - 尼克·泰勒（他/他）—— May 5 5月5日



原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on May 15

Part of a new series! Feel welcome to dip in and weigh in on a past question.

Let's say I've never used Ruby before. Can anyone give the run down of what the language does and why you prefer it? Feel free to touch on drawbacks as well.

Syed Ali Hussnain - May 13

Nick Taylor (he/him) - May 5
item.author: ben

item.title: Azure 应用服务 | Azure App Service
item.description:
发表于什么是 Azure 应用服务？

您可以使用任何您喜欢的语言进行编码，包括 .NET、.NET Core、Java、Ruby、Node.js、PHP 或 Python。 基于 Windows 和 Linux 的系统都可以轻松运行和扩展应用程序。

网络应用

  • 移动应用
  • 逻辑应用
  • 如何创建 Azure Web App第 1 步：登录 Azure 门户

    第二步：点击创建应用服务


    • 第 3 步：填写名称，即资源组，它是一个容器，用于保存 Azure 解决方案的相关资源。 资源组可以包含解决方案的所有资源，也可以只包含您想要作为组和应用服务计划管理的资源，还可以选择区域和应用服务计划，最后单击查看并创建。


    第 5 步：等待您的应用完全部署并返回到您的应用资源组。

    部署后，您可以通过从左侧菜单栏中的选项中进行选择来维护和管理您的应用程序，其中包括允许您的函数应用程序运行称为“槽”的不同实例的部署槽，

    部署中心，使您能够从 GitHub、bitbucket、azure repo 和本地 git 部署您的应用程序，

    更改服务计划，它是一组供 Web 应用运行的计算资源，定价层决定了您获得的应用服务功能以及您为该计划支付的费用。 有几类定价层：

    共享计算：免费和共享都与其他客户的应用共享您的应用的资源池。 这些层将 CPU 配额分配给在共享资源上运行的每个应用程序，并且资源不能横向扩展。

    专用计算：Basic、Standard、Premium、PremiumV2 和 PremiumV3 层在专用 Azure VM 上运行应用。 只有同一应用服务计划中的应用共享相同的计算资源。 层级越高，可用于横向扩展的 VM 实例就越多。

    隔离：此层在专用 Azure 虚拟网络上运行专用 Azure VM。 它在计算隔离之上为您的应用程序提供网络隔离。 它提供了最大的横向扩展能力。

    消费：此层仅适用于功能应用。 它根据工作负载动态扩展功能。

    应用服务身份验证/授权是一项功能，它允许您的应用登录用户，而无需更新后端的任何代码。 它使保护您的应用程序和处理每个用户的数据变得简单。



    原文:

    slarry07

    Posted on May 15

    what is Azure App Service?

    Azure App Service is a web application, REST API, and mobile back-end hosting service that runs on HTTP. You can code in any language you like, including.NET, .NET Core, Java, Ruby, Node.js, PHP, or Python. Both Windows and Linux-based systems make it easy to run and scale applications.

    Azure app service includes 4 application development and hosting environment.

    1. Web App
    2. Mobile App
    3. Logic Apps
    4. API Apps

    How to Create Azure Web App step 1: Log in to the Azure portal

    step 2: Click on Create App service


    step 3: Fill in the name, the resource group which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. The resource group can include all the resources for the solution, or only those resources that you want to manage as a group and app service plan, also select region and app service plan and finally click on review and create.


    step 5: wait for your app to fully deploy and return to your App Resource group.

    after deployment, you can maintain and manage your app by selecting from the options at the left-hand menu bar which, includes the Deployment slot that allows your function app to run different instances called "slots",

    Deployment centerwhich enables you to deploy your app from GitHub, bitbucket, azure repo, and local git,

    Change service plan which is a set of compute resources for a web app to run, The pricing tier which determines what App Service features you get and how much you pay for the plan. There are a few categories of pricing tiers:

    Shared compute: Both Free and Shared share the resource pools of your apps with the apps of other customers. These tiers allocate CPU quotas to each app that runs on the shared resources, and the resources can't scale out.

    Dedicated compute: The Basic, Standard, Premium, PremiumV2, and PremiumV3 tiers run apps on dedicated Azure VMs. Only apps in the same App Service plan share the same compute resources. The higher the tier, the more VM instances are available to you for scale-out.

    Isolated: This tier runs dedicated Azure VMs on dedicated Azure Virtual Networks. It provides network isolation on top of compute isolation to your apps. It provides the maximum scale-out capabilities.

    Consumption: This tier is only available to function apps. It scales the functions dynamically depending on workload.

    App Service Authentication / Authorization is a feature that allows your app to sign in users without having to update any code on the backend. It makes it simple to secure your application and work with per-user data.
item.author: slarry07

item.title: 臭名昭著的二和问题。 (DSA Series 3) （DSA 系列 3） | The infamous Two Sum problem. (DSA Series 3)
item.description:
奥卢米德塞缪尔

发表于给定一个整数数组 nums 和一个整数目标，返回两个数字的索引，使它们加起来等于目标。 您可能会假设每个输入都只有一个解决方案，并且您可能不会两次使用相同的元素。

您可以按任何顺序返回答案。

  • 例子
  • 输入：nums = [2,7,11,15]，目标 = 9
  • 输出：[0,1]
  • 解释：因为 nums[0] + nums[1] == 9，所以我们返回 [0, 1]。
  • 输入：nums = [3,2,4]，目标 = 6
  • 输出：[1,2]
  • 输入：nums = [3,3]，目标 = 6
  • 输出：[0,1]

解决方案

  • 这只有在物品被分类时才有效
  • 让有两个指针，一个指向数组的开头（低），另一个指向数组的结尾（高）。
  • 虽然低 < 高，
  • 如果低和高的值之和 = 目标，则返回索引
  • 如果低值和高值的总和 > 目标，则将高指数减 1
  • 如果低值和高值的总和 < 目标，则将低值增加 1
 function twoSum ( array , target ){ let low = 0 ; let high = array . length - 1 ; while ( low < high ) { const sum = array [ low ] + array [ high ]; if ( sum === target ) return [ low , high ]; if ( sum > target ) high -- ; else if ( sum < target ) low ++ ; } }

方法二

/** * * Input: nums = [2,7,11,15], target = 9 * Output: [0,1] * Initialize a map, * loop through array, * subtract currentItem from target to get compliment, * if compliment exist in map, return [currentItmIdx, compliment[]] * else add map[curentItem] = idx * @param {*} array * @param {*} target */ function twoSumMethod2 ( array , target ){ const map = {}; for ( let i = 0 ; i < array . length ; i ++ ) { let com = target - array [ i ]; if ( map [ com ] !== undefined ) return [ i , map [ com ]] else map [ array [ i ]] = i ; } return - 1 }

测试示例

常量数 = [2,7,11,15]，目标 = 9；
常量数 = [3,3]，目标 = 6

无论如何，如果您有更好的方法来解决这个问题，您可以在评论中删除您的解决方案。 我不是专家。 只是大声学习。

不要忘记喜欢，分享和发表评论。 :) :)



原文:

OlumideSamuel

Posted on May 15

Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

You can return the answer in any order.

  • Example
  • Input: nums = [2,7,11,15], target = 9
  • Output: [0,1]
  • Explanation: Because nums[0] + nums[1] == 9, we return [0, 1].
  • Input: nums = [3,2,4], target = 6
  • Output: [1,2]
  • Input: nums = [3,3], target = 6
  • Output: [0,1]

Solution

  • THIS WILL ONLY WORK IF ITEMS ARE SORTED
  • let there be two pointers, one point to the beginning of array (low), and the other to the end of array (high).
  • while low < high,
  • if the sum of the value at low and high = target, return the indices
  • if the sum of the values at low and high > target, reduce high index by 1
  • if the sum of the values at low and high < target, increase the low by 1 
function twoSum(array, target){ let low = 0; let high = array.length - 1; while (low < high) { const sum = array[low] + array[high]; if (sum === target) return [low, high]; if (sum > target) high--; else if (sum < target) low++; }
}

Method Two

/** * * Input: nums = [2,7,11,15], target = 9 * Output: [0,1] * Initialize a map, * loop through array, * subtract currentItem from target to get compliment, * if compliment exist in map, return [currentItmIdx, compliment[]] * else add map[curentItem] = idx * @param {*} array * @param {*} target */
function twoSumMethod2(array, target){ const map = {}; for (let i = 0; i < array.length; i++) { let com = target - array[i]; if(map[com] !== undefined) return [i, map[com]] else map[array[i]] = i; } return -1
}

Test Examples

const nums = [2,7,11,15], target = 9;
const nums = [3,3], target = 6

-

Anyway, if you have a better way of solving this, you can drop your solution in the comments. I'm no expert. Just learning aloud.

Don't forget to like, share and drop a comment. :)
item.author: olumidesamuel_

item.title: 什么是`this`？ 技术债！ | What is `this`? Technical debt!
item.description:
不仅在 DEV 中，而且在整个网络上都有大量关于 JavaScript 中的this的文章。 这是因为对于初学者来说， this是关于 JavaScript 最令人困惑的话题之一，即使他们有其他编程语言的经验。

在本文中，我们将讨论您可能希望避免this情况的一些原因。

隐式不好

它已经为您定义好了。 您可能会想一秒钟“那太好了”，但您必须考虑到这取决于您在代码中的位置， this会发生变化，因此您不能 100% 确定它的当前值是多少。

 console . log ( this ); // Will log `globalThis`. const object = { shouldLogObject () { console . log ( this ); }, }; object . shouldLogObject (); // Will log `object` [ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( object . shouldLogObject ); // Will log `globalThis` 3 times (???) const aFunction = function () { console . log ( this ); }; aFunction (); // Will log `globalThis` const arrowFunction = () => console . log ( this ); // Will log `globalThis` aFunction . call ( " foo " ); // Will log `"foo"`

现在，让我们看看如果我们不使用this会是什么样子：

 console . log ( " hello " ); // Will log `"hello"`. const object = { shouldLogArgument ( argument ) { console . log ( argument ); }, }; object . shouldLogArgument ( " foo " ); // Will log `"foo"` [ 1 , 2 , 3 ]. map ( object . shouldLogArgument ); // Will log `1`, `2` and `3` const aFunction = function ( argument ) { console . log ( argument ); }; aFunction ( " foo " ); // Will log `"foo"` // Will log the value of `argument`: const arrowFunction = argument => console . log ( argument ); aFunction ( " foo " ); // Will log `"foo"`

几乎相同数量的代码，但现在很清楚所有内容的作用，因为我们对此很明确。

那里的一些帖子告诉你，通过使用函数附带的一些方法来解决这类问题是“容易的”： bind 、 call和apply 。 对我来说，这就像把补丁放在补丁上，而不是修复根本问题。 假设我们有一个函数，它接受一个名为onEvent的回调：

 // with plain functions: onEvent ( doSomething ); // readable, no issues here // but with class methods: onEvent ( instance . doSomething ); // will break if it uses `this` // to "solve" that: onEvent ( event => instance . doSomething ( event )); // verbose // or onEvent ( instance . doSomething . bind ( instance )); // wth?! // or when you write the class itself: class Example { constructor () { this . doSomething = this . doSomething . bind ( this ); // 🤮 } }

如果你不使用类...

我已经在 DEV 上发表了一篇关于为什么不需要类来编写代码的文章，这篇文章有点相关，主要是因为this在使用class时非常常见。 您可能会说，如果您使用静态方法和类似的东西，那么您就不会使用this ，但我对此的回答是：那您为什么还要使用class呢？ 您可以将这些静态方法作为模块中的函数并直接使用它们。

那么学习this是什么没用吗？

不，您肯定需要知道它是如何工作的，更不用说浏览器和使用类的服务器中有很多本机 API，并且知道如何处理它们在使用this时很有用。 我的观点是你应该避免使用this来改善你的生活和其他开发人员的生活的代码。

结束的想法

我写这篇文章主要是因为一些解释this是什么的人，当它实际上很糟糕时，也试图像好的东西一样“卖”它，所以如果你在野外遇到this ，去学习如何处理它，但要避免尽可能在您编写的代码中使用它。

就是这样。 就是那个帖子。

如果您同意，我很乐意阅读this让您感到困惑的场景，如果您不同意，请评论您认为依赖这样的隐含值是个好主意的this 。

感谢您抽出宝贵的时间阅读！

干杯!



原文:

There are a plethora of articles not only here in DEV, but all over the web, about what is this in JavaScript. That's because this is one of the most confusing topics about JavaScript for juniors, even if they are experienced in other programming languages.

There are lots of answers to the question "What is this?", but from my point of view the answer is just one: "Technical debt", and that's because, for me, anything written using this, can and should be written without it.

In this article, we will go over some of the reasons you might want to avoid this as well.

Implicit isn't good

this is an implicit value, which means that you're not defining it as an argument of your functions, as a variable, or anything like that. It's already defined for you. You might think for a second "that's great", but you have to consider that depending on where you are in your code, this changes, so you can't be 100% certain about what's its current value.

console.log(this); // Will log `globalThis`. const object = { shouldLogObject() { console.log(this); },
}; object.shouldLogObject(); // Will log `object` [1, 2, 3].map(object.shouldLogObject); // Will log `globalThis` 3 times (???) const aFunction = function () { console.log(this);
}; aFunction(); // Will log `globalThis` const arrowFunction = () => console.log(this); // Will log `globalThis` aFunction.call("foo"); // Will log `"foo"`

Now, let's see how this looks like if we don't use this:

console.log("hello"); // Will log `"hello"`. const object = { shouldLogArgument(argument) { console.log(argument); },
}; object.shouldLogArgument("foo"); // Will log `"foo"` [1, 2, 3].map(object.shouldLogArgument); // Will log `1`, `2` and `3` const aFunction = function (argument) { console.log(argument);
}; aFunction("foo"); // Will log `"foo"` // Will log the value of `argument`:
const arrowFunction = argument => console.log(argument); aFunction("foo"); // Will log `"foo"`

Is almost the same amount of code, but now is pretty clear what everything does because we are being explicit about it.

The "solutions" are even worst

Some posts out there tell you that is "easy" to solve this kind of issue, by using some methods that come with a function: bind, call and apply. To me, that's like putting patches over patches instead of fixing the root issue. Let's say that we have a function that takes a callback called onEvent:

// with plain functions:
onEvent(doSomething); // readable, no issues here // but with class methods:
onEvent(instance.doSomething); // will break if it uses `this` // to "solve" that:
onEvent(event => instance.doSomething(event)); // verbose // or
onEvent(instance.doSomething.bind(instance)); // wth?! // or when you write the class itself:
class Example { constructor() { this.doSomething = this.doSomething.bind(this); // 🤮 }
}

If you don't use classes...

I already have a post here on DEV about why you don't need classes in order to code, and this post is kinda related, mainly because this is extremely common when working with class. You might say that if you work with static methods and stuff like that, then you don't use this, but then my answer to that is: Why are you even using class then? You could just have those static methods be functions in a module and use them directly.

So is it useless to learn what this is?

No, you definitely need to know how this works, not to mention that there are lots of native APIs in the browser and the server that uses classes, and knowing how to deal with this is useful when working with them. My point is that you should avoid code that uses this to make your life and the lives of other devs better.

Closing thoughts

I wrote this article mainly because some folks that explain what this is, also try to "sell it" like something good, when it actually sucks, so go and learn how to deal with it if you encounter one this in the wild, but avoid it as much as possible in the code you author.

That's it. That's the post.

If you agree I would love to read scenarios where this messed with you, and if you disagree please comment on the reasons you believe is a good idea to rely on an implicit value like this.

Thanks for taking the time of reading!

Cheers!
item.author: lukeshiru

item.title: go-retry - 引入允许您从进程中间重试的 Go 包 | go-retry - Introduction of a Go package that allows you to retry from the middle of a process
item.description:
这是 2021 年 11 月在zenn.dev上最初以日语发表的一篇文章的翻译。

为什么我开发它

由于有时我们想一步一步地执行通信、安装等一系列过程，并在每一步重试，所以我们决定将重试功能泛化，并将其分离为一个包。

起初，实施只是重试，直到现在取消上下文，但正如预期的那样，我们现在正在实施指数退避和最大重试次数等最低要求。

用法

要使用这个包，只需在retry.DoWithContext中设置retry.DurationFunc ，它设置重试间隔，然后为您希望执行的过程的每个步骤传递一个函数。

 package main import ( "context" "log" "github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry" ) func main () { // Prepare context for cancellation. ctx , cancel := context . WithCancel ( context . Background ()) defer cancel () errs := retry . DoWithContext ( ctx , // Retry 5 times while doing Exponential Backoff. retry . ExponentialBackoff ( 5 ), // Separate the process you want to execute into functions // for each step and pass them as arguments. func ( ctx context . Context ) error { // If something occurs that cannot be retried, // the process is canceled with retry.Canceled. if failed { return retry . Canceled } return nil }, // If the previous step is successful, // proceed to the next step. func ( ctx context . Context ) error { return nil }, ) // Check all errors that occurred during execution. for _ , err := range errs { log . Print ( err ) } }

什么是指数退避？

简单来说，就是根据进程失败的次数成倍增加重试间隔的算法。 如果您查看细节，您可以找到一个很好的解释，即延长重试间隔的必要性，但我认为它通常是为了“避免处理冲突”或“减少负载”而实现的。

如何调整重试间隔

目前这个包实现了标准的retry.ExponentialBackoff和 retry.Duration]( https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry#Duration )。

将重试间隔调整设为一个函数的原因部分是因为我们喜欢让它成为一个简单的可定制实现，但主要是因为指数退避有许多不同的实现。 例如，当前的retry.ExponentialBackoff实现是通过使用随机值的算法实现的，例如200ms + (20ms ~ 100ms) 。 通过如下实现您自己的函数，可以在不使用随机值的情况下使用指数退避。

 func ExponentialBackoff ( maxRetries int ) retry . DurationFunc { return func ( retries int ) time . Duration { if maxRetries >= 0 && maxRetries < retries { return - 1 } return time . Duration ( math . Pow ( 2 , float64 ( retries )) * 100 ) * time . Millisecond } }

まとめ

这个包的设计和实现是为了与通信过程、安装过程、数据库等以及具有交互界面的 CLI 一起使用。 它被设计成易于集成到各个地方，所以如果你喜欢，请使用它。

我也欢迎任何关于retry.DurationFunc和其他贡献的补充或建议。



原文:

This is a translation of an article originally published in Japanese on zenn.dev in November 2021.

Why I developed it

Since there were times when we wanted to execute a series of processes such as communication and installation step by step and retry at each step, we decided to generalize the retry function and separate it as a package.

https://github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry

At first, the implementation was just to retry until the context is canceled for now, but as expected, we are now implementing minimum requirements such as Exponential Backoff and maximum retry count.

Usage

To use this package, simply set retry.DurationFunc, which sets the retry interval, in retry.DoWithContext, and then pass a function for each step of the process you wish to execute.

package main import ( "context" "log" "github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry"
) func main() { // Prepare context for cancellation. ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background()) defer cancel() errs := retry.DoWithContext(ctx, // Retry 5 times while doing Exponential Backoff. retry.ExponentialBackoff(5), // Separate the process you want to execute into functions // for each step and pass them as arguments. func(ctx context.Context) error { // If something occurs that cannot be retried, // the process is canceled with retry.Canceled. if failed { return retry.Canceled } return nil }, // If the previous step is successful, // proceed to the next step. func(ctx context.Context) error { return nil }, ) // Check all errors that occurred during execution. for _, err := range errs { log.Print(err) }
}

What is Exponential Backoff?

Simply explained, it is an algorithm that exponentially increases the retry interval according to the number of process failures. You can find a good explanation of the necessity of lengthening the retry interval if you look into the details, but I think it is often implemented for "avoiding processing conflicts" or "reducing the load.

https://cloud.google.com/iot/docs/how-tos/exponential-backoff

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/api-retries.html

How to adjust retry interval

The retry.DurationFunc interface can be used to implement any retry interval algorithm. Currently this package implements the standard retry.ExponentialBackoff and retry.Duration](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry#Duration).

The reason for making the retry interval adjustment a function is partly because we like to make it a simple customizable implementation, but mostly because there are many different implementations of Exponential Backoff. For example, the current implementation of retry.ExponentialBackoff is implemented with an algorithm using random values such as 200ms + (20ms ~ 100ms). Exponential Backoff can be used without using random values by implementing your own function as follows.

func ExponentialBackoff(maxRetries int) retry.DurationFunc { return func(retries int) time.Duration { if maxRetries >= 0 && maxRetries < retries { return -1 } return time.Duration(math.Pow(2, float64(retries))*100)*time.Millisecond }
}

まとめ

This package is designed and implemented for use with communication processes, installation processes, databases, etc., as well as CLI with interactive interfaces. It is designed to be easy to integrate into various places, so please use it if you like.

I would also welcome any additions or suggestions for retry.DurationFunc and other contributions.
item.author: kenkyu

item.title: NPM 库使用 WP REST API 获取 WordPress 帖子的字符数列表 | NPM Library to get a list of WordPress posts' character counts using the WP REST API
item.description:
新藤由纪

发表于我创建了一个 npm 库来使用 WordPress 提供的 REST API 检索在 WordPress 上发布的所有文章的字符数。
名称是wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount 。

检查 GitHub 存储库以获取详细信息。
安装和代码示例如下。

安装：

 npm install wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount # or yarn add wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount

例子：

 const getWordcountList = require ( ' wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount ' ); ( async () => { try { const response = await getWordcountList ( ' your wordpress url ' ); console . log ( response ); } catch ( e ) { console . error ( e ); } })();

什么是 WP REST API？

为了简要解释此库中使用的 WP REST API，WordPress 为 REST API 提供了以下端点。

这可以在 WordPress 端禁用，但如果您没有对设置进行任何更改，则当前可能默认启用。

此 REST API 可用于检索有关已发布文章的信息。

例如，您可以检查响应头中的值x-wp-total以获取目标 WordPress 页面的数量等。您可以通过多种方式使用它，因此阅读此文档会很有趣。

使用 WP REST API 的代码示例

这是wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount中实际使用 WP REST API 的地方。

这表明使用 WP REST API 非常容易。

 const targetUrl = ` ${ url } /wp-json/wp/v2/posts` ; const response = await fetch ( targetUrl ); const wpTotalPageCount : number = response . headers . get ( ' x-wp-total ' );

可以通过像这样向端点your_wordpress_url/wp-json/wp/v2/posts发送请求来检索有关公共帖子的信息。

从这个示例中，您可以看到此 API 易于使用。

通过查看官方文档可以找到各种信息。

让我们使用 WP REST API 构建各种有用的工具！



原文:

Yuki Shindo

Posted on May 15

I have created an npm library to retrieve the character count of all articles published on WordPress using the REST API provided by WordPress.
The name is wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount.

Check the GitHub repository for details.
https://github.com/shinshin86/wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount

Installation and code examples are as follows.

Install:

npm install wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount
# or
yarn add wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount

Example:

const getWordcountList = require('wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount'); (async () => { try { const response = await getWordcountList('your wordpress url'); console.log(response); } catch (e) { console.error(e); }
})();

What is WP REST API?

To briefly explain the WP REST API used in this library, WordPress provides the following endpoints for REST APIs.

https://developer.wordpress.org/rest-api/

This can be disabled on the WordPress side, but is probably currently enabled by default if you have not made any changes to your settings.

This REST API can be used to retrieve information about published articles.

For example, you can check the value x-wp-total in the response header to get the number of target WordPress pages, etc. You can use it in various ways, so it would be interesting to read this document once.

(I actually use x-wp-total in my npm library)

Code sample using WP REST API

Here is where the WP REST API is actually used in wp-rest-api-posts-wordcount.

This shows that it is very easy to use the WP REST API.

const targetUrl = `${url}/wp-json/wp/v2/posts`;
const response = await fetch(targetUrl);
const wpTotalPageCount: number = response.headers.get('x-wp-total');

It is possible to retrieve information about public posts by sending a request to the endpoint your_wordpress_url/wp-json/wp/v2/posts like this.

response.headers.get('x-wp-total') to get the number of pages in WordPress.

From this sample, you can see that this API is easy to use.

Various information can be found by checking the official documentation.

Let's build all sorts of useful tools using the WP REST API!
item.author: shinshin86

item.title: 基本 NPM 命令 | Basic NPM Commands
item.description:
比科代码

发表于搭建好n node.js开发环境后，需要了解node包管理器npm的一些基本命令。 以下是最常用的。

安装 npm

您可以从他们的官方网站https://nodejs.org/en/download/安装 node.js 安装完 node 后，您可以通过运行以下命令来检查 node 和 npm 的版本：

标志

初始化项目的 package.json 文件。

使用-y标志跳过问题并使用默认值创建 package.json。

安装 package.json 中列出的依赖项。

速记： npm i

从 npm 注册表安装包。

速记： npm i <package>

在你的机器上全局安装一个包。

 npm install <package-name@version-number>

安装特定的软件包版本而不是最新版本。

就像命令本身所暗示的那样，它会卸载已安装的软件包。

速记：npm 卸载

npm uninstall -g <package>

从您的机器上卸载一个全局包。

列出升级的过期包

将指定的包更新到可用的最新版本。 如果未指定包，它会更新每个包。

检查有多少缓存条目可用。

速记： npm up <package>

清除 npm 缓存

对所有可用的项目包执行安全检查。

将全局包更新到可用的最新版本。

以树状结构列出所有已安装的包及其版本，以及它们的依赖关系。

获得一定深度的包裹。 您安装的包将位于深度 0。其依赖项将位于深度 1，进一步的依赖项将位于深度 2，依此类推。

 npm view <package> <version>

显示有关指定包的可用详细信息。 如果未设置版本，则默认为最新版本。

如果在 package.json 中作为脚本对象的属性找到，则执行指定的脚本。

尝试为提供的术语显示相应的文档页面。

获取特定命令的帮助。

 npm help-search < command >

搜索 npm 文档以获取帮助。

这会从 package-lock.json 安装包而不更新依赖关系树。

重建包

查找主要版本

查找次要版本

查找补丁版本

结论

此列表并不详尽。 我欢迎在下面的评论中涉及这项工作的任何批评、建议和贡献。

chijioke augustine - chijioke 奥古斯丁 - May 14 5月14日



原文:

BikoCodes

Posted on May 15

After setting up n node.js development environment, you need to know some basic commands of node package manager npm. The followings are the most commonly used ones.

npm stands for node package manager.

Installing npm

npm comes with the node.js. You can install node.js from their official website https://nodejs.org/en/download/ After installing node, You can check the version of node and npm by running the following commands:

Flags

-S is the same as --save, and -D is the same as --save-dev.

Initializes the package.json file of a project.

Use the -y flag to skip the questions and to create a package.json using default values.

Installs the dependencies listed in package.json.

Shorthand: npm i

Installs a package from the npm registry.

Shorthand: npm i <package>

Installs a package globally on your machine.

npm install <package-name@version-number>

Install a specific package version rather than the most latest version.

Like the command itself suggests, it uninstalls packages that are already installed.

Shorthand: npm uninstall 

npm uninstall -g <package>

Uninstalls a global package from your machine.

List the outdated packages for upgrade

Updates the specified package to the latest version available. If package is not specified, it updates every package.

Check how many cache entries are available.

Shorthand: npm up <package>

Clears the npm cache

Performs a security check for all the available project packages.

Updates a global package to the latest version available.

Lists all the installed packages and their versions, along with their dependencies in a tree structure.

To get packages of a certain depth. The packages installed by you will be in the depth 0. Its dependencies will be in the depth 1 and further dependencies will be in the depth 2 and so on.

npm view <package> <version>

Shows available details about the specified package. If the version is not set, it defaults to the latest version.

Executes the specified script, if found as a property of the script object in package.json.

npm CLI has built in help command

-S is the same as --save, and -D is the same as --save-dev.

Tries to show the appropriate documentation page for the term provided.

To get help for a particular command.

npm help-search <command>

To search npm documentation for help.

This installs packages from package-lock.json without updating the dependency tree.

Rebuild packages

Find a major version

Find a minor version

Find the patch version

Conclusion

This list is not exhaustive. I welcome any criticism,advice and contribution involving this work in the comments below.

chijioke augustine - May 14
item.author: bikocodes

item.title: 中奖卡（DSA系列） | Winning Card (DSA Series)
item.description:
奥卢米德塞缪尔

发表于获奖卡
在纸牌游戏中，每个玩家将获得一组随机牌。 玩家将他们的一张获胜牌扔到桌子上，拥有最高牌的玩家获胜。

获胜的牌是在一组牌中只存在一次的牌，也是最高的牌。

给定一组整数卡片，返回获胜玩家的卡片。 如果没有找到这样的卡，则返回 -1。

  • 示例 1：
  • 输入：卡片 = [[5,7,3,9,4,9,8,3,1], [1,2,2,4,4,1], [1,2,3]]

  • 输出：8

  • 示例 2：

  • 输入：卡片 = [[5,5], [2,2]]

  • 输出：-1

解决方案

  • 循环遍历二维数组，
  • 在第二个循环中，如果项目存在于哈希集中，则删除之前出现的 i。 否则添加到哈希集
  • 循环遍历哈希集以获取最大数字
function winningCard ( multiArray ) { let map = new Set (); multiArray . forEach ( item => { for ( let i = 0 ; i < item . length ; i ++ ) { if ( map . has ( item [ i ])) { map . delete ( item [ i ]) } else map . add ( item [ i ]) } }) if ( map . size === 0 ) return - 1 ; let highest = 0 ; for ( let i of map ) { if ( i > highest ) { highest = i } } return highest ; }

无论如何，如果您有更好的方法来解决这个问题，您可以在评论中删除您的解决方案。 我不是专家。 只是大声学习。

不要忘记喜欢，分享和发表评论。 :) :)



原文:

OlumideSamuel

Posted on May 15

Winning Card
In a card game, each player will be given a set of random cards. Players will throw on the table their one winning card, the player with the highest card wins.

A winning card is the card that only exists once in the set of cards, and the highest one.

Given an array of sets of integers cards, return the card of the winning player. Return -1 If no such card is found.

  • Example 1:
  • Input: cards = [[5,7,3,9,4,9,8,3,1], [1,2,2,4,4,1], [1,2,3]]

  • Output: 8

  • Example 2:

  • Input: cards = [[5,5], [2,2]]

  • Output: -1

Solution

  • Loop through the 2 dimensional array,
  • On second loop, if item exist in hashset, delete prev occurence of i. Else add to hashset
  • loop through hashset for the highest number 
function winningCard(multiArray) { let map = new Set(); multiArray.forEach(item => { for (let i = 0; i < item.length; i++) { if (map.has(item[i])) { map.delete(item[i]) } else map.add(item[i]) } }) if (map.size === 0) return -1; let highest = 0; for (let i of map) { if(i > highest) { highest = i } } return highest;
}

Anyway, if you have a better way of solving this, you can drop your solution in the comments. I'm no expert. Just learning aloud.

Don't forget to like, share and drop a comment. :)
item.author: olumidesamuel_

item.title: 改变一切的会议 | DevoxxUK - afterparty. The conference which changed EVERYTHING
item.description:
几天前，作为伦敦 Java 社区的代表，我是DevoxxUK的幸运参与者之一。

老实说，我并没有期望太多。 为什么？ 我想，就像我参加过的以前的会议一样，我不会学到从书本或课程中学不到的东西。 我错了。 大错特错。 绝对错误。

我参加了我一生中最好的 2 场 IT 会议：Benjamin Muskalla 的“开源效应”和 Kevlin Henney 的“Ch-ch-changes: A talk about human and code
凯夫林·亨尼 '。 最重要的是，我与 CSM 的一位了不起且鼓舞人心的 Sal Kimmich 进行了交谈。

以前的会议甚至不如 DevoxxUK 那样令人惊叹。

那么发生了什么变化？ 为什么 DevoxxUK 会议与其他会议如此不同？ 答案很简单。 会议过去和将来都很棒。

一个参数发生了变化。

我改变了。

以前我参加会议是为了参加有关技术的会议并带回家一些赃物。 哦，我是多么的低效……

这一次，在聆听绝对令人惊叹的本杰明·穆斯卡拉（Benjamin Muskalla）时，我意识到我在那里是因为人们。

我会记住那一刻，作为我人生的转折点。 真的。

我意识到我周围的所有这些人，包括演讲者在内，都比单纯的技术知识更有趣、更有价值。 我可以在书籍或互联网上阅读更多关于技术的信息，但这些人对技术、我们的工作方式和生活方式有不同的、有时是矛盾的、几乎总是非常动态的看法。 书籍将永远保持静止，比人更静止。

我不是说我们不读书。 我们绝对必须读书。 但我们不应该牺牲与人交谈的好处。 我们也不应该牺牲阅读书籍的好处。 只是像我这样的开发人员更喜欢读书而不是与其他人交谈，甚至与其他开发人员交谈。 那就是要去的地方。 那是成长的地方。 它甚至可能是获得智慧的地方，或者至少是其中的一部分。

请让我自己解释一下。 开发人员通常从书籍或编码实践中学习。 在最好的情况下，他们从合作中学习。 最幸运的人从结对编程或 XP 中学习。 或者我是这么想的。

好吧，我是对的，也是错的。

虽然所有这些学习方式都很有效，但在会议上与人交谈和倾听要好得多。 为什么？ 好吧，这些不是随机的人。 他们是顶尖的专业人士。 是的，贵公司的人也是专业人士。 但是，您已经对您的同事了解很多，并且可能在知识方面与他们同步。

新人会给你带来新的想法、新技术、新的建议。 他们甚至可能会告诉你一些你从未注意过的事情，因为你认为你已经知道这个话题了。 或者你可能不知道这个话题。

你会学到很多以前学不到的东西，因为你的大脑在“非会议模式”下工作。

如果你很幸运，你将了解技术和职业的新视野和新的未来方向。

例如，我认为我了解如何以及为什么要做开源。 好吧，参加@bmuskalla的会议让我明白我对为开源做出贡献的理解是非常错误的。 更重要的是，开源社区中很多备受尊敬的人都是这样开始的。

在接下来的几周里，我将发布我在 DevoxxUK 参加的会议。 我很想在这篇文章中写下@bmuskalla的会议，但我担心缺少关键细节。 因此，我会等待并观看视频记录，当它们准备好时。 之后，我会根据我最喜欢的 DevoxxUK 会议写一些文章。

我在参加会议时学到的技巧和窍门：

  • 与其他与会者讨论您和他们感兴趣的事情。 这些可能是不同的主题，但你会惊讶于你能学到多少
  • 现在 - 说得更多。 尽量少吃午餐，尽量少分心。 说话。 到。 人民。
  • 查看人们的徽章并在 LinkedIn 上关注他们。 尤其是演讲者，那些在展位的人，以及那些在会议期间提问的人。 请记住，即使是“普通”与会者也是“预选”的，因为他们足够活跃，可以找到参加会议的方法
  • 在展位上，与公司代表聊聊他们公司所做的事情。 在他们的产品中找到你了解的东西并开始提问。 这些人参加会议是因为他们热爱自己的工作。 这些人真的很喜欢问好问题。 所以问“但是你比这样那样的数据库提供商有什么优势”是一个完全有效的问题
  • 尽量少写，尤其是在会议期间。 问题是写作会分散注意力，即使您实际上是在笔记本电脑或手机上打字。 我并不是说你不应该做笔记或发推文，但请理解这些都是相当分散注意力的奢侈品
  • 尝试强加积极的倾听。 向演示者写下问题，这样您就可以在最后提出这些问题。
  • 为演示文稿拍照。 至少对我来说，看一下本杰明演讲的照片，我立刻想起了他在说什么

最后一个。 我不记得在与所有精彩的会议与会者交谈后，我是什么时候如此高兴。 我很开心，充满活力。

我为我的第一个开源项目做出了贡献。 谁能想到。

人们。 你摇滚。

PS Special thanks to @antonarhipov for his help with terminology used in my post. PS 特别感谢@antonarhipov对我帖子中使用的术语的帮助。



原文:

Few days ago I was one of the lucky attendees of DevoxxUK as a representative of the London Java Community.

To be honest, I didn’t expect much. Why? I thought that similar to the previous conferences I had attended, I will not learn something which cannot be learned from the books or courses. I was wrong. So wrong. Absolutely wrong.

DevoxxUK was absolutely amazing. I have attended the 2 best IT sessions in my life: Benjamin Muskalla's 'The Open Source Effect' and Kevlin Henney's 'Ch-ch-changes: A talk about humans and code
by Kevlin Henney'. On the top of it, I have spoken to an amazing and inspiring Sal Kimmich, CSM.

Previous conferences were not even closely same amazing as that DevoxxUK.

So what has changed? Why was DevoxxUK conference so different from all others? The answer is simple. The conferences were and will be great.

One parameter has changed.

I have changed.

Previously I was attending conferences to attend the sessions about technology and bring home some swag. Oh, how inefficient I was…

This time, while listening to an absolutely amazing Benjamin Muskalla, I realized that I am there because of people.

I will remember that moment as a turn point in my life. Really.

I realized that all these people around me, including the speaker are much more interesting and valuable than just pure technical knowledge. I can read more about tech in the books or internet, but these people will have different, sometimes contradicting and almost always very dynamic views on the technology, on the ways we work and on the ways we live. Books will always remain static, a way more static than people.

I am not saying that we shall not read book. We absolutely must read books. But we should not sacrifice the benefits of speaking to people. Neither shall we sacrifice the benefits of reading the books. It is just that developers like myself prefer reading books to talking to other people, even to other develoepers. Covid-19 pandemic made it worse, but the problem had been there long before teh pandemic.

That is the place to be. That is the place to grow. It might even be a place for getting wisdom, or at least some of it.

Please let me explain myself. Developers normally learn from books or coding practice. In the best case, they learn from collaboration. The most luckiest learn from pair programming or XP. Or so I thought.

Well, I was right and wrong at the same time.

While all these ways of learning are efficient, speaking and listening to people at the conferences is much better. Why? Well, these are not random people. They are top noch professionals. Yes, people in your company are also professionals. However, you already know a lot about your colleagues and probably you have synched with them in terms of knowledge.

New people will bring you new ideas, new technologies, new advice. They might even tell you something which you never pointed your attention to, because you thought you already know the topic. Or maybe you were not aware about the topic.

You will learn a ton of things which you couldn't learn before, because your brain was working in a "non-conference-mode".

If you are very lucky, you will learn about new horizons and new upcoming directions in technology and careers.

For example, I thought I understand how and why to do Open Source. Well, attending @bmuskalla ’s session made me understand that I was VERY wrong in my understanding of contributing to Open Source. Benjamin and Sal have explained everybody, that it is actually extremely easy to get into OpenSource. Even more, a lot of highly respected people in the OpenSource community have started just like that.

During the upcoming weeks I will post about the sessions which I have attended at the DevoxxUK. I am very tempted to write about @bmuskalla ’s session in this post, but I am concerned with missing crucial details. Therefore I will wait and watch the video recordings, when they are ready. After that, I will write a number of articles based on the DevoxxUK sessions I liked most.

Tips and tricks I learned attending the conference:

  • Speak with other attendees about stuff which interests you and them. These might be different topics, but you will amazed how much can you learn
  • Now - speak even more. Minimize your lunch, minimize every distraction you can. SPEAK. TO. THE PEOPLE.
  • Take a look at people’s badges and follow them on LinkedIn. Especially the speakers, those who are at the boothhs, and those who are asking questions during the sessions. Remember, even “generic” attendees are “preselected” as they were active enough find a way to get to the conference
  • While at the booths, chat to the company representatives about stuff which their company does. Find something in their product which you have understanding about and start asking questions. These people are at the conference because they love their jobs. These people really enjoy good questions. So asking “but what is your edge over such and such database provider” is a perfectly valid question
  • Try to write less, especially during the sessions. The problem is that writing distracts, even if you actually are typing on your laptop or on your phone. I am not saying that you shall not take notes or make tweets, but please understand that these are quite a distractive luxuries
  • Try to impose active listening. Write down questions to the presenter, so you can ask these questions at the end.
  • Take pictures of the presentation. At least for me, taking a look at the photo of Benjamin’s presentation immediately helped me to recall what he was speaking about

And the last one. I do not remember when I have been that happy as after talking to all those wonderful attendees of the conference. I am happy and full of energy.

And I have contributed to my first Open Source project. Who could think.

People. You rock.

P.S. Special thanks to @antonarhipov for his help with terminology used in my post.
item.author: nikitakoselev
